It checks: (a) gh api rulesets contains review-gate as required; (b) required-checks.txt matches live; (c) all three carriers exit≠0 on invalid token.'
2. Add a story-7 precondition check: 'Before executing the enforce-flip, run `gh pr list --base main --state open | wc -l` and assert output is 0 (or == 1 for the flip PR itself).'
3. Add a step that regenerates required-checks.txt from the live ruleset and commits it, so SC3 file-vs-live parity is mechanically verifiable.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; first paragraph; SC1–SC6
SUCCESS CRITERIA section (SC1–SC6) and DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub … AND by the named E-series/V-series experiments')
findings.finding: The plan names experiments (E2, E5, E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8, E-compute-hash) as test validators, but the ticket body does not describe what each experiment actually does, what observable it produces, or how RED-before-GREEN ordering is established for any of them. The experiments are defined in 'docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md', which is not reproduced here. A reader who does not have that document cannot verify whether failure paths (token expiry, scope-strip, allowlist bypass, re-run warm-hit) are genuinely exercised. Additionally, the COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER is mentioned generically in the DELIVERY PRINCIPLES but is not visible in the SC text or any per-story slot — the actual observables that trigger a stop are absent from this document.
findings.suggested_edit: For each named experiment/V-series that backs a SC, add a one-line description of: (a) what action is taken, (b) what the expected observable is, and (c) what constitutes RED. At minimum, embed the COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER definition for Story 7 (the enforce-flip) directly in the plan, since it is the single hard-to-reverse step.

findings.location: Title, NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT, SUCCESS CRITERIA, SEQUENCING
SUCCESS CRITERIA (SC1–SC6) vs. plan body (stories 1–8)
findings.finding: The SUCCESS CRITERIA reference individual story numbers (stories 1–8) and the enforce-flip (story 7) and UX story (story 8) in the SEQUENCING section, but the actual story descriptions are not present in this ticket — they presumably live in child tickets or in the referenced handoff document. This creates two gaps: (1) There is no visible mapping from each SC to the specific described work that satisfies it (no orphan-criteria check is possible without the story content). (2) SC6 mentions 'descriptive PR1 titles; sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle' and 'ADR-0020 updated with execution results', but neither item appears in the SEQUENCING block or any other section of this plan — they look like orphan criteria with no corresponding described work. Additionally, the term 'review-gate' is used interchangeably with 'required check' in SC1/SC3 but is also conflated with the broader 'review-coverage-invariant.yml' CI wrapper concept in SC2, which may or may not be the same artifact.
findings.suggested_edit: Either inline the story titles/summaries into the SEQUENCING section or add a cross-reference table mapping each SC to the story(ies) that deliver it. Clarify whether 'review-gate' (the required check name) and 'review-coverage-invariant.yml' (the CI wrapper) are the same check or distinct components. Confirm that SC6's UX/ADR items have a home in story 8 or another named story.

findings.location: DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('warn-first … single enforce-flip (final story) … hard-gated') vs. SEQUENCING ('story 7 (enforce-flip) depends on 1–6 ALL verified') vs. SC2 ('fail-closed on precondition/token failure under enforce mode')
findings.finding: Two potential internal tensions are present: (1) DELIVERY PRINCIPLES states 'warn-mode checks are advisory (no team impact)' for stories 1–6, yet SC2 requires all three CI wrappers to 'fail-closed on precondition/token failure under enforce mode'. If warn mode is genuinely advisory (exit-0 on token failure), then SC2 cannot be verified until story 7 flips enforcement — but SC2 is listed as a pre-condition for story 7. The plan is silent on how SC2 is validated in warn mode before the flip. (2) The SEQUENCING section says 'stories 1–4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4', but story 3 is absent from the dependency chain entirely, creating ambiguity about whether story 5 also needs story 3 or whether story 3 is a true parallel with no downstream blockers.
findings.suggested_edit: Clarify how SC2's fail-closed behavior is tested without actually enabling enforce mode (e.g., a dry-run flag, a shadow run against a test branch). Explicitly state whether story 3 must complete before story 5 or is genuinely independent.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA section (SC1–SC6) and DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub … AND by the named E-series/V-series experiments')
findings.finding: The plan references named experiments (E2, E5, E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8, E-compute-hash, E6') as test vehicles but does not define what those experiments actually do, what their pass/fail oracle is, or who runs them. Without access to docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md or the experiment definitions, it is impossible to confirm whether failure-path coverage, RED-before-GREEN ordering, or boundary cases (e.g., token expiry, scope-strip, ledger cold-start, the allowlist-only PR2 carrying unreviewed content) are actually exercised. The COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER per story is mentioned as a principle but no concrete observable threshold is shown in this document. The structural greps and admin/non-admin PAT distinction are healthy signals, but because all behavioral assertions are delegated to external references, the plan's self-contained testability is thin.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC, inline a one-line experiment summary: what is injected, what output is asserted, and the RED-before-GREEN step (e.g., 'SC2: run with scope-stripped token → confirm exit non-zero BEFORE running with valid token → confirm exit 0'). This avoids dependency on an external doc for reviewers and agents.

findings.location: Title, NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT, SUCCESS CRITERIA, SEQUENCING
Multiple sections
findings.finding:
Several coherence issues exist between criteria and description:
1. ORPHAN CRITERIA: SC5 ('behavior-bearing surfaces removed from review-gate allowlist') and SC6 ('UX correctness fixes: descriptive PR1 titles; sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle') reference concrete implementation steps (allowlist editing, PR1 title logic, lifecycle changes) that are not described anywhere in the plan body — they are only named in the success criteria. Story 8 is called out as 'UX, independent' in SEQUENCING but its content is never elaborated.
2. ORPHAN DESCRIPTION: The DELIVERY PRINCIPLES section introduces R8 RED and the 'live<->file skew window' concept, but no success criterion validates R8's fail-closed behavior independently (SC3 references it but only within the live-parity guard context; it is not a standalone verifiable criterion).
3. TERMINOLOGY INCONSISTENCY: The plan uses 'review-gate' and 'llm-review' as check names and also references 'merge-pipeline-checks' and 'review-coverage-invariant.yml', 'dangling-references.yml', 'ruleset-invariants.yml' as carriers. The relationship between 'review-gate' (the required check) and these three YAMLs is implied but never stated explicitly — is 'review-gate' a separate job, or the composite result of the three carriers?
4. NEAR-DUPLICATE: SC1 ('single always-runs required check … fail-closes unless every SHA … proven reviewed') and SC2 ('all review-enforcing CI wrappers fail-closed on precondition/token failure') overlap significantly in their assertion that the gate is fail-closed; the distinction (precondition failure vs. logic failure) is implied but not called out.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a short 'Architecture sketch' section that (a) names the three YAML carriers and explains their role relative to the 'review-gate' required check, and (b) expands story 8 with at least one acceptance criterion for the UX deliverables (PR1 title and lifecycle). Annotate SC1/SC2 with a parenthetical distinguishing logic-failure from precondition-failure to eliminate the near-duplicate overlap.

findings.location: SEQUENCING ('stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel … story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified') vs. DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Warn-first: every story ships its enforcing logic in warn/advisory mode … validated on live PR2 traffic BEFORE any enforce-flip') vs. SC3 ('the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap is reflected on BOTH required-checks.txt and the live ruleset')
findings.finding:
Three potential cross-section tensions:
1. SEQUENCING vs. DELIVERY PRINCIPLES: SEQUENCING says stories 1–4 'may proceed in parallel', but DELIVERY PRINCIPLES requires each story to be 'validated on live PR2 traffic BEFORE any enforce-flip.' If multiple stories modify the same live ruleset or required-checks.txt concurrently, the warn-mode validation window for each story may be contaminated by another story's in-flight changes. The plan does not describe how parallel execution is isolated.
2. SC3 vs. DELIVERY PRINCIPLES: SC3 requires the 'merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap' to be reflected on the live ruleset, while DELIVERY PRINCIPLES warns that 'R8 RED is repo-wide during the live<->file skew window' and requires 'no other PR2 in flight.' This hard mutual-exclusion constraint is only mentioned in DELIVERY PRINCIPLES, not surfaced in SC3 or in the enforce-flip story's own criteria — making it easy to miss at execution time.
3. COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER is declared as a per-story artifact in DELIVERY PRINCIPLES but is absent from the SUCCESS CRITERIA section and the SEQUENCING block, creating ambiguity about whether a triggered course-correction invalidates dependent stories already in flight.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Parallelism guard' note to SEQUENCING clarifying which shared artifacts (ruleset, required-checks.txt) are write-locked during a story's live-validation window, preventing concurrent stories from touching them. Surface the 'no other PR2 in flight' constraint as a prerequisite bullet in SC3 (or the enforce-flip story) so it is not silently inherited from DELIVERY PRINCIPLES.

findings.location: Plan body: 'files/symbols' are referenced by workflow file names (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml, required-checks.txt) and docs paths (docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md, docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md); the new check name 'review-gate' is named but no source file path is given for it.
findings.finding: The plan names three existing CI workflow files and one text manifest (required-checks.txt) as targets, and introduces a new 'review-gate' required check, but does not specify: (a) which workflow file(s) will implement the new 'review-gate' check (is it a new .github/workflows/review-gate.yml? a job added to an existing file?); (b) the coverage-ledger storage mechanism (file path, artifact name, or cache key) referenced in SC1/SC4; (c) which ruleset ID or name in GitHub is being modified (live ruleset for 'main'). Callers/consumers that may need updating — e.g., any status-check references in CODEOWNERS, branch-protection rules outside the named ruleset, or scripts that parse required-checks.txt — are not enumerated. Without codebase access it cannot be confirmed whether these exist, hence AMBIGUOUS rather than FAIL.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Files / symbols touched' section listing: the new workflow file path for review-gate, the ledger storage path/key, the ruleset name/ID, and a note confirming no other branch-protection rules reference the old 'merge-pipeline-checks' check name.

findings.location: SC1–SC6 vs. plan body; 'story 8 (UX)' reference vs. SC6; experiment labels E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8
findings.finding: Most SCs map back to named stories or experiments, so the overall coherence is good. Two gaps: (1) SC6 mentions 'PR1 descriptive titles' and 'sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle' as UX correctness fixes, but the plan body only says 'story 8 (UX) is independent and may land any time' — there is no description of what those UX fixes actually are, so SC6 has acceptance criteria for described work that has no body detail. (2) The experiment/variant labels (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8) are cited as verifiers but are defined entirely in an external document (docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md). If that document changes or is unavailable, the acceptance criteria become unverifiable. These are minor gaps rather than outright failures.
findings.suggested_edit: Inline at least a one-sentence description of each E-series/V-series label used in SC1–SC6, or add a lookup table so the plan is self-contained. Also expand the body description of story 8 to define the UX acceptance criteria that SC6 references.

findings.location: Title vs. body; NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; SUCCESS CRITERIA; SEQUENCING
SEQUENCING section: 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel… story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 is independent'
findings.finding: The plan explicitly identifies this as an epic with 8 child stories, provides a clear dependency DAG, isolates the single hard-to-reverse step (story 7, the enforce-flip) behind all prior stories, and tags story 8 as independent. Decomposition is vertical (each story ships observable value: warn → verify → flip) rather than horizontal-layer. Appropriately structured for an epic.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA preamble: 'epic closes only when ALL are met + completion-verifier PASS'; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES: 'Live-verify each story on real GitHub'; COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER named per story
findings.finding: The plan requires a 'completion-verifier PASS' but does not describe what this verifier is — its name, location, invocation command, or output format. Each story is said to name a COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER, but no story body is present in this plan (stories 1–8 are referenced but not described), so it cannot be confirmed that triggers are actually defined. The SCs themselves are well-formed and binary-testable (check RED/GREEN, ledger hit/miss, ruleset diff), so end-state reachability is good if the story bodies exist. Marking AMBIGUOUS because the completion-verifier and story bodies are in external documents not reproduced here.
findings.suggested_edit: Either inline the completion-verifier definition (script path + expected output) or add a pointer to its exact location. Confirm each story body exists in the referenced external documents before agent execution begins.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES; SC1–SC2
findings.finding: The problem and beneficiary are clearly stated: the team (every committer to 'main') currently relies on a flow-enforced LLM review that can be bypassed or silently skipped; the work hardens this into a ruleset-enforced, fail-closed gate. The failure modes being fixed (silent green on token expiry, skip-to-success on allowlisted PRs, per-file vs. per-SHA coverage gaps) are concretely named.

findings.location: Plan description: 'Source of truth: docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md ... and docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md'; SC2 references 'review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'; SC3 references 'required-checks.txt'
findings.finding: The plan names several concrete file targets (the three .yml workflow files, required-checks.txt, ADR-0020, the ruleset itself) and two source-of-truth documents, but does NOT enumerate the full edit set per story. Specifically: (1) The workflow files that will have fail-closed wrapper logic added are named in SC2 but it is unclear which stories touch which files. (2) The 'live ruleset' swap (SC3) implies a GitHub API/UI side-effect whose blast radius (e.g., any branch-protection rules that overlap, any required-status-checks wired elsewhere) is not acknowledged. (3) The allowlist modification described in SC5 is not traced to a file name. (4) 'coverage ledger' warm-hits (SC4) implies some cache/storage artifact whose location is unspecified. Without access to the live codebase these cannot be confirmed as real files, hence AMBIGUOUS rather than FAIL.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Files / artefacts touched' table per story (or at epic level) listing: each workflow YAML, the required-checks.txt path, the allowlist config file, the coverage-ledger store location, and the ADR. Note any overlapping branch-protection rules and confirm the ruleset swap is the single authoritative enforcement point.

findings.location: SC4: 'a commit whose diff is entirely within the event-sourced ticket store is exempt'; SC5: 'behavior-bearing surfaces (docs/**, skills, behavior-driving config) are removed from the review-gate allowlist'; plan body: 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 depends on 1-6'
findings.finding: The six success criteria are each traceable to language in the plan body, and no SC appears wholly orphaned. However three coherence gaps exist: (1) SC4 references an 'event-sourced ticket store' exemption that is never defined or explained in the plan body — it is used as a known term but has no glossary entry or description. (2) SC5 references an 'allowlist' that is never introduced as a concept in the description section; it appears only in the SC. (3) 'stories 1–8' are referenced in the SEQUENCING paragraph but only story 8 (UX) is described by name; stories 1–7 have no titles or body text in the plan, leaving their content implicit. This means several SC items (e.g., 'sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle' in SC6, 'descriptive PR1 titles' in SC6) have no corresponding described work.
findings.suggested_edit: Define 'event-sourced ticket store' in a glossary or description paragraph. Introduce the 'allowlist' concept in the description before using it in SC5. Add at minimum a one-line summary per story (1–7) so each SC can be mapped to a concrete story.

findings.location: Title vs. plan body and all SCs

SEQUENCING paragraph: 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 (enforce-flip) depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 (UX) is independent'
findings.finding: This ticket is an epic that references at least 8 child stories but provides no body text for any of them — not even titles for stories 1–7 (story 8 is named 'UX'). The dependency graph is stated but the stories are not decomposed into separate tickets or even described inline. An executing agent has no clear unit of work to act on: the plan is simultaneously too large (8+ stories, multiple independent tracks, a live ruleset flip, runbook, and documentation update) and under-specified (no story-level acceptance criteria, no story-level file targets). The 'enforce-flip' (story 7) is called 'hard-to-reverse' in the plan, underscoring why it needs its own leaf ticket with its own gate conditions rather than being one of eight unnamed stories.
findings.suggested_edit: Convert this epic into a parent ticket that links to 8 separate child story tickets. Each child should carry: a one-sentence goal, its own acceptance criteria, the specific files it touches, its COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER, and its E/V experiment verification reference. The parent ticket retains the SCs as epic-level completion criteria only.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA: 'epic closes only when ALL are met + completion-verifier PASS'; SC1: 'Verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED [E2/E13]'; SC3: 'the R8 live-parity guard fail-closes under enforce so PAT expiry/scope-strip/drift cannot silently disable it [E6', V0.11]'
findings.finding: The plan defines a 'completion-verifier PASS' as the final gate but does not specify what the completion-verifier is, who runs it, or what its pass/fail output looks like. The E-series and V-series experiment references (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8) are relied upon throughout the SCs as verification proxies but are never described in the plan body — their content and pass criteria are only derivable from the referenced external documents. If those documents are unavailable to the executing agent, the verification steps are unexecutable. Additionally the 'warn-first → enforce-flip' transition lacks an explicit observable threshold: what quantity or duration of clean live PR2 traffic is required before the enforce-flip is permitted?
findings.suggested_edit: Inline or summarize each experiment (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15) as a two-line pass/fail description directly in the relevant story or SC. Define the completion-verifier: its name, how it is invoked, and what constitutes PASS. Add a numeric threshold for the warn-mode validation window (e.g., 'N PRs or X days of clean traffic with zero false-positives before story 7 may proceed').

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT paragraph and SC1/SC2
SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC6 and DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub')
findings.finding: Multiple acceptance criteria require live GitHub interactions that cannot be completed within one agent session without real external state. Specifically: SC1 requires blocking a live PR2 on an allowlisted-only PR with un-reviewed content ('Verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED'); SC2 requires a scope-stripped/expired token to make the gate RED on live CI runs; SC3 requires the live main ruleset to reflect required-checks.txt changes AND the R8 live-parity guard to fail-close under real PAT expiry; the DELIVERY PRINCIPLES explicitly state 'Live-verify each story on real GitHub (admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads).' None of these can be completed by an agent in a single working session without real GitHub credentials, live repo state, and ongoing PR traffic. There is also no simulation/mock fallback defined for these live validations. Additionally, SC4's 'no flaky-check team block' and SC6's 'docs reflect the final enforced architecture' are partially unmeasurable as written — 'flaky-check' has no defined threshold, and 'reflect' has no specific diff or checklist specified.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC that requires live GitHub validation, add an explicit in-session proxy: e.g., 'verified via a recorded dry-run against a fork/test repo with a scoped test PAT' or 'verified by inspecting CI log artifacts from the last run against the named experiment.' For 'no flaky-check team block' (SC4), define a measurable threshold (e.g., 'zero false-positive gate failures across 5 consecutive clean PR2 runs'). For SC6, enumerate the specific doc sections that must be updated.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT, SC1, SC2, SC3, and 'Source of truth: docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md … docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md'
findings.finding: The plan makes several unverified codebase/environment assertions that an executor must confirm before acting: (1) It asserts that review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, and ruleset-invariants.yml exist and currently lack fail-closed behavior under token failure (SC2) — this can only be confirmed against the live codebase. (2) It assumes a 'review-gate' check can be inserted as a single always-runs required check and that the current ruleset supports this without structural changes to GitHub rulesets configuration (SC1/SC3). (3) SC3 references 'required-checks.txt' as an existing file that will be updated — its existence, format, and current contents are unverified in the plan text. (4) SC4 references an 'event-sourced ticket store' whose current exemption mechanism (allowlist? path filter?) is assumed to exist but not described. (5) The plan references experiments V0.1–V0.12 and E-series as already-run, implying their results are recorded in docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md — but whether that document exists and is complete is unverified. (6) The 'merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap' (SC3) implies 'merge-pipeline-checks' is currently a required check — not confirmed in plan text.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a preflight checklist story (or expand story 1) that explicitly verifies: (a) existence and current content of the three named yml files; (b) current required checks on the main ruleset (screenshot or API output); (c) existence and schema of required-checks.txt; (d) the current allowlist/path-filter mechanism for the ticket store exemption. Each subsequent story should reference that preflight output rather than asserting these preconditions.

findings.location: SC1 ('a single always-runs required check `review-gate`'), SC2 (three separate CI wrappers: review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml), SC3 ('R8 live-parity guard')
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new 'review-gate' check on top of three existing CI workflow wrappers (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml) plus an 'R8 live-parity guard.' Without seeing the current codebase it is impossible to confirm whether this represents genuine consolidation or the addition of a fourth/fifth enforcement layer on top of already-existing gate logic. If any of the three existing workflows already encapsulates the per-SHA coverage check, adding 'review-gate' as a new wrapper around them would be a Rule-of-Three violation (a new abstraction at exactly one call-site). The plan does not explain why the existing workflows cannot themselves be made the required ruleset check, which raises a potential NIH/over-engineering concern. This is marked AMBIGUOUS because the actual content of the three yml files is not visible in the plan.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a one-paragraph rationale in the plan (or the ADR) explaining why 'review-gate' must be a new check rather than promoting one of the three existing workflows to a required ruleset check. If 'review-gate' is purely an orchestrator/fan-in, state that explicitly and describe what it adds over simply requiring review-coverage-invariant.yml directly.

findings.location: SEQUENCING section, SUCCESS CRITERIA, DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('completion-verifier PASS')
findings.finding: The plan references a 'completion-verifier' as the final gate for epic closure ('epic closes only when ALL are met + completion-verifier PASS') but does not define what the completion-verifier is, how it is run, what its inputs/outputs are, or who/what runs it. This is a critical gap in the termination condition. Additionally, the sequencing description ('stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 is independent') references eight stories whose scope, acceptance criteria, and COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGERS are not enumerated in this ticket — they are implied to exist elsewhere. An executor reading only this plan cannot determine when each individual story is done, only when the epic is done (and even then only partially, given the undefined completion-verifier). The gap between 'what we'll do' and 'what done looks like' is therefore unresolved at the story level. SC4's 'coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs' also lacks a measurable definition of 'warm-hit' (cache hit rate? latency threshold?).
findings.suggested_edit: Either: (a) inline the eight story definitions (scope + per-story acceptance criteria + COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER) directly into this ticket, or (b) add explicit links to the eight child tickets and state that this ticket is the epic shell only — an executor should not begin work on this ticket directly but should execute child tickets. For the completion-verifier, specify: what script/workflow it is, what inputs it checks, and what a PASS output looks like (e.g., 'run scripts/verify-epic-close.sh; PASS = exit 0 with all SC1–SC6 checks green in the log'). For SC4 'warm-hits', define a concrete measurable (e.g., 'second run of review-gate on the same SHA set completes in <N seconds with cache-hit log line present').

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC6 and DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub')
findings.finding:
Multiple acceptance criteria require live GitHub operations that cannot be completed within a single working session by an automated agent:

1. SC1 requires 'Verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED' — this demands creating or observing a real PR2 on the live repo and watching the GitHub Actions run to completion.
2. SC2 requires verifying behavior 'in ALL THREE carriers' with a 'scope-stripped/expired token' — a live token manipulation experiment that needs real GitHub secrets and CI runs.
3. SC3 requires 'live ruleset requires review-gate and retains llm-review' and a 'live-parity guard' check — verifying actual GitHub ruleset state via API or UI is out-of-band for an agent without live admin PAT provisioned in-session.
4. SC4 references 'no flaky-check team block' across 're-runs' — inherently multi-run, multi-session validation.
5. SC5 requires observing 'a docs-only PR now requires review; an image/lockfile PR still skips' — again real PR traffic.
6. DELIVERY PRINCIPLES require 'Live-verify each story on real GitHub (admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads)' — PAT provisioning is an out-of-session human operation.

None of the SCs define a simulation-safe or offline-verifiable proxy (e.g., a dry-run mode, a mock GitHub API, or a pre-recorded fixture). The plan explicitly distinguishes live verification from simulation and treats live verification as mandatory ('NON-NEGOTIABLE'), making offline or single-session completion impossible for these criteria.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC that requires live GitHub validation, add a parallel 'unit-verifiable proxy' sub-criterion that an agent can confirm in-session (e.g., 'CI script exits non-zero when REVIEW_TOKEN env var is empty', verified by a local shell test). Separate live smoke-tests into a distinct 'post-deploy verification checklist' owned by a human operator, clearly labelled as out-of-scope for automated agent completion. This preserves the rigor intent while making agent-completable work clearly bounded.

findings.location: SC1, SC2, SC3, SC6 and 'Source of truth: docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md … docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md'
findings.finding:
The plan rests on several unverified assertions about the codebase and environment that an executor would need to confirm before acting:

1. 'a single always-runs required check `review-gate` exists' (SC1) — the plan asserts this check is already present or will simply be created, but neither the current CI YAML structure nor whether a job named `review-gate` already exists (possibly under a different name) is verified in the plan text.
2. 'three carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml' (SC2) — the plan assumes these three workflow files exist and currently run on PR-to-main; this is not confirmed anywhere in the plan.
3. 'live `main` ruleset requires `review-gate` and retains `llm-review`' (SC3) — the plan treats the current ruleset state as known, referencing a 'required-checks.txt' file, but does not verify the current live ruleset matches that file.
4. 'the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap is reflected on BOTH required-checks.txt and the live ruleset' (SC3) — presupposes a 'merge-pipeline-checks' required check currently exists in the live ruleset, unconfirmed.
5. 'the coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs' (SC4) — assumes a 'coverage ledger' caching mechanism already exists with defined warm/cold hit semantics.
6. 'behavior-bearing surfaces (docs/**, skills, behavior-driving config) are removed from the review-gate allowlist' (SC5) — presupposes an allowlist mechanism already exists with these entries.

Without access to the live codebase and GitHub ruleset, none of these can be confirmed from the plan text alone.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Prerequisites / Baseline Audit' section that explicitly lists each assumed artifact (workflow file names, ruleset state, allowlist file path, ledger implementation) with a verification step: 'Run X command / inspect Y file to confirm Z exists before starting story N.' This converts implicit assumptions into explicit gating checks the executor can perform at session start.

findings.location: SC1 ('single always-runs required check `review-gate`'), SC3 ('required-checks.txt AND the live ruleset'), SC2 ('ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml')
findings.finding:
Two potential over-engineering concerns are present but cannot be confirmed without the live codebase:

1. Three separate CI workflow files (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml) all individually implement fail-closed token/precondition checks (SC2). If these checks are largely identical boilerplate, this is a Rule-of-Three violation — the same sentinel logic is copy-pasted across three carriers rather than extracted to a shared action or script called by all three. Whether a shared helper already exists or the checks are truly distinct cannot be determined from plan text alone.
2. A 'required-checks.txt' file is maintained alongside the live GitHub ruleset (SC3). This creates a dual source of truth for required checks — the canonical live ruleset and a file-based mirror — which must be kept in sync via the 'R8 live-parity guard'. Maintaining a file-based mirror of live ruleset state is an additional abstraction layer that adds complexity (drift risk, sync tooling) for a single use case (parity auditing). If the GitHub API is directly queryable in CI, this file may be unnecessary.
findings.suggested_edit:
1. For the three-carrier fail-closed pattern: extract the common token/precondition sentinel into a single composite GitHub Action (e.g., `.github/actions/fail-closed-sentinel/action.yml`) and have all three workflows call it — unless the checks are genuinely distinct enough to preclude sharing.
2. For required-checks.txt: evaluate whether the parity guard can query the live ruleset directly via GitHub API (e.g., `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets`) and compare in-memory, eliminating the file-based mirror entirely. Only retain the file if offline/air-gapped audit is a real requirement.

findings.location: SEQUENCING section, SUCCESS CRITERIA, and 'completion-verifier PASS'
findings.finding:
Several gaps exist between the described steps and the stated end-state, and the termination condition is underspecified:

1. 'Completion-verifier PASS' (epic closing condition) is referenced but never defined in the plan. What is the completion-verifier? What does it check? What constitutes PASS? Without this definition, the agent has no concrete terminal condition.
2. Story 7 ('enforce-flip') is described as 'the only hard-to-reverse step' and 'hard-gated' on stories 1–6 ALL verified, but the plan does not describe what 'hard-gated' means mechanically — is there a CI check, a manual approval step, a checklist gate? The verification mechanism for the gate itself is absent.
3. SC6 requires 'ADR-0020 updated with execution results' — this implies post-execution documentation that must be written after live verification is complete, creating a circular dependency: the epic can't close until ADR is updated, but ADR can't be updated until execution results are known, which requires live ops.
4. COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGERs are mentioned as required for each story but are not listed in the plan body. Without them, the executor has no defined stop condition when things go wrong.
5. The SEQUENCING note states 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified' — but story 3 is never mentioned in the dependency chain. It is unclear whether story 3 is a prerequisite for story 7 or can be skipped.
6. R8 ('repo-wide RED during live<->file skew window') is referenced in SC3 and the enforce-flip runbook but the runbook itself is not present in the plan — it is only described as something 'the enforce-flip story carries', meaning it will be written later, creating a forward dependency on undefined content.
findings.suggested_edit:
1. Define the 'completion-verifier' concretely: name the script/check and its PASS criteria (e.g., 'run scripts/verify-epic-complete.sh; exits 0 iff all six SC checks pass in CI').
2. Add a 'Story 7 Gate Checklist' with enumerated, binary-verifiable items (not prose) that must all be true before the enforce-flip PR is merged.
3. Add story 3 explicitly to the story 7 dependency list or explicitly note it is not a prerequisite.
4. Include the R8 runbook inline (or as a linked, already-written doc) rather than promising it will be written later.
5. List COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGERs for each story in the plan body, not just in the delivery principles.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA section (SC1–SC6) and DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub')
findings.finding: The plan references a rich set of named experiments (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8, E-compute-hash) as the testing evidence for each success criterion, but the plan body itself does not describe what those experiments actually do, what their RED-before-GREEN ordering looks like, what boundary cases they cover, or what a FAIL result looks like vs. a PASS result. The experiments live in docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md, which is referenced but not inlined. An executor cannot verify whether the test plan is complete, covers failure paths (expired token, scope-stripped token, mid-flight skew window), and follows RED-before-GREEN without reading that external document. The warn-first / enforce-flip sequencing is described at a high level, but the specific observable outputs that confirm RED then GREEN for each check are absent from this ticket.
findings.suggested_edit: For each success criterion, add a one-sentence description of what each cited experiment does and what a PASS vs. FAIL output looks like (e.g., 'E13: submit a PR2 containing an un-reviewed SHA with only allowlisted authors; assert review-gate status == failure'). This makes the test plan self-contained and auditable without opening the external doc.

findings.location: SC2 ('ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'), SC3 ('merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap'), SC6 ('docs reflect the final enforced architecture; ADR-0020 updated')
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new top-level required check (`review-gate`) that aggregates three existing CI files AND retains `llm-review`, meaning there are now at least four enforcing surfaces that all must fail-closed. Without seeing the current workflow files it is ambiguous whether the three existing carriers already share a common wrapper/helper that centralises fail-closed logic, or whether fail-closed logic will be copy-pasted into each. If the latter, this is a minor NIH/proliferation risk (Rule-of-Three: three call-sites duplicating the same precondition-check pattern suggests a shared action/script is warranted). Cannot confirm from plan text alone.
findings.suggested_edit: State explicitly whether the fail-closed precondition logic will be extracted into a shared composite action or reusable script, or whether it will be duplicated per carrier and why that is acceptable.

findings.location: Title vs. plan body overall

Multiple locations: SEQUENCING ('stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4…'), SC3 ('live ruleset requires review-gate and retains llm-review'), DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub (admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads)')
findings.finding:
Three distinct areas leave the executor without enough detail to proceed without stopping:
1. STORY CONTENT IS ABSENT: The plan references 'stories 1–8' and their dependency graph but never defines what any individual story's scope is. An executor cannot act on 'story 5 depends on 1+2+4' without knowing what work stories 1–4 contain. This is a hard blocker for decomposition.
2. ADMIN PAT SCOPE: The plan says 'admin PAT: provision/read' but does not specify which GitHub token scopes are required (e.g. `repo`, `admin:org`, `admin:repo_hook`, etc.), where the PAT is stored (Actions secret name), or who provisions it.
3. ENFORCE-FLIP RUNBOOK IS REFERENCED BUT NOT PRESENT: SC3 / DELIVERY PRINCIPLES mention 'the enforce-flip story carries the runbook (land the swap with no other PR2 in flight — R8 RED is repo-wide during the live<->file skew window)' but the runbook itself is deferred to the story, which is not defined in this ticket. An executor hitting the enforce-flip step would have to invent the runbook.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Stories' section that lists each story's title, scope, and acceptance criteria in at least two or three sentences each. Specify the exact GitHub token scopes and Actions secret name for the admin PAT. Include at minimum a skeleton runbook for the enforce-flip step (pre-conditions to check, sequence of API calls, rollback step).

findings.location: SEQUENCING section; overall plan length and referenced artifacts
findings.finding: This is explicitly described as an epic ('this epic translates plan Phase 2…') with 8 child stories, but none of those stories are defined in the ticket body. The sequencing graph is present (story 5 depends on 1+2+4, story 7 depends on 1–6, etc.) but the children themselves are absent — they have no scope, no acceptance criteria, and no definition of done. As written, this is simultaneously an epic shell (too large to execute as one unit) and a leaf task (it provides no child tickets to act on). This means an executor has no coherent unit of work to execute in a single session, and a reviewer cannot assess whether the decomposition is sensible.
findings.suggested_edit: Either (a) add a child-story definition section with at minimum a title, one-paragraph scope, and done-condition for each of the 8 stories; or (b) convert this into a pure epic/tracking ticket and create separate child tickets for each story, linking them here.

findings.location: SC4 ('a commit whose diff is entirely within the event-sourced ticket store is exempt'), SC5 ('genuinely-inert artifacts … and the ticket store remain exempt'), DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('warn-first … enforce-flip'), SC6 ('flow preserved + documented; ADR-0020 updated with execution results')
findings.finding:
Two coherence/terminology issues:
1. ORPHAN CRITERION – SC6 mentions 'descriptive PR1 titles; sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle' and 'ADR-0020 updated with execution results,' but neither of these is described anywhere in the plan body (no story, no step, no design note covers PR1 title formatting or sprint-umbrella lifecycle behaviour). These appear to be criteria without corresponding described work.
2. TERMINOLOGY DRIFT – The plan uses 'two-tier flow', 'merge-pipeline-checks', 'review-gate', 'llm-review', and 'R8' without a single definition or glossary entry. 'merge-pipeline-checks' and 'review-gate' are clearly different check names but their relationship (is 'review-gate' the replacement for 'merge-pipeline-checks'?) is only implied in SC3. An executor could mis-implement the swap. Also, 'warn mode' and 'advisory mode' are used interchangeably (DELIVERY PRINCIPLES: 'warn/advisory mode') — pick one.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a short Glossary or Terminology section defining: two-tier flow, review-gate, llm-review, merge-pipeline-checks, R8, warn/enforce mode, ticket store. For SC6, add a description of what PR1 title changes and sprint-umbrella lifecycle work is required, or remove those items from SC6 if they are out of scope.

findings.location: SC2: 'all review-enforcing CI wrappers fail-closed on precondition/token failure under enforce mode … in ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'
findings.finding: The plan asserts by name that three specific workflow files (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml) already exist as 'CI wrappers' and already contain (or will trivially receive) a fail-closed token-check path. It also asserts in SC3 that 'required-checks.txt' exists as the live-parity source of truth, and in SC4 that 'the coverage ledger' already supports warm-hits across re-runs. None of these structural claims are verifiable from the plan text alone. If any of the three named YAMLs does not exist, is named differently, or does not currently have a precondition-check hook, the executor will hit a silent gap mid-story. Similarly, the 'coverage ledger warm-hit' in SC4 implies a caching/persistence mechanism that the plan does not define or confirm exists.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Precondition checks' section at the start of each story that lists: (a) verify each named YAML exists at the stated path, (b) confirm required-checks.txt exists and is the authoritative source used by the R8 guard, (c) confirm the coverage-ledger cache key and warm-hit behavior. If any are absent, the story must include a 'create/add' sub-task rather than assuming them.

findings.location: SC1, SC2, SC3, SC4, SC5, SC6 — all success criteria; also 'Live-verify each story on real GitHub (admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads)'
findings.finding: Every success criterion requires live GitHub operations: SC1 requires a real PR2 to be BLOCKED by the ruleset; SC2 requires a real token to be scope-stripped and the gate observed to go RED; SC3 requires observing a live ruleset change on GitHub; SC5 requires a real docs-only PR to be verified as requiring review. These are out-of-session, real-human/live-infra validations that an agent cannot complete autonomously in a single working session. The plan explicitly requires 'verified live' for SC1–SC5, but provides no simulation path, no mocked GitHub API, and no local-runner substitute. The phrase 'no real-user validation' condition in the criterion is not met.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC, add an in-session verifiable proxy: e.g. for SC1, a dry-run script that calls the GitHub Checks API with a known SHA and asserts the gate returns 'failure'; for SC2, a unit/integration test that invokes the wrapper with a deliberately invalidated token env-var and asserts non-zero exit; for SC3, a script that reads the live ruleset via API and diffs against required-checks.txt. Mark the live-GitHub observation steps as a separate 'post-deploy smoke test' runbook rather than part of the machine-verifiable acceptance criteria.

findings.location: 'a single always-runs required check review-gate exists' (SC1) alongside the retained 'llm-review' check (SC3: 'retains llm-review'); also the three separate CI wrapper files for fail-closed enforcement (SC2)
findings.finding: The plan preserves two required checks (review-gate AND llm-review) and three separate CI YAML files each carrying their own fail-closed token/precondition wrapper. Without seeing the existing workflow structure it is not possible to assert this is redundant, but the surface-area risk is real: if llm-review and review-gate overlap in what they gate, the dual-required-check pattern may be NIH duplication. Similarly, three separate wrapper implementations of 'fail on bad token' instead of one shared composite action is a Rule-of-Three violation if those wrappers are structurally identical. Cannot confirm without the live codebase.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a one-paragraph rationale in the plan (or in ADR-0020) explaining why llm-review must be retained alongside review-gate rather than subsumed, and whether the three fail-closed wrappers should share a composite GitHub Action rather than be copy-pasted.

findings.location: Multiple locations: 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 (enforce-flip) depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 (UX) is independent' — but individual stories are never listed or described; also 'Source of truth: docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md … docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md'
findings.finding: The plan references 8 numbered stories and their dependency graph but never defines what any individual story does. An executor reading this ticket alone has no actionable decomposition — they must go read two external documents to learn what Stories 1–8 contain, what files each touches, and what 'warn-mode' vs 'enforce-mode' means operationally. The plan also leaves the enforce-flip (Story 7) runbook detail entirely in an external ADR ('land the swap with no other PR2 in flight — R8 RED is repo-wide during the live<->file skew window') with no inline steps. This pushes real design decisions to the executor. Additionally, 'behavior-driving config' (SC5) is undefined — an executor must guess which files belong to this category vs the 'genuinely-inert' category.
findings.suggested_edit: Inline at minimum a one-sentence scope statement for each of the 8 stories (what it creates/modifies/deletes and in which files). Inline the Story 7 runbook steps. In SC5, give a concrete list of glob patterns or directory paths that are 'behavior-bearing' vs 'genuinely inert' rather than leaving it as prose classification.

findings.location: DELIVERY PRINCIPLES: 'Warn-first: every story ships its enforcing logic in warn/advisory mode … BEFORE any enforce-flip'; vs SC2: 'all review-enforcing CI wrappers fail-closed on precondition/token failure under enforce mode' verified as a success criterion of the epic (not just Story 7)
findings.finding: The warn-first principle states that every story ships warn/advisory first and the enforce-flip is a single hard-gated final step (Story 7). But SC2 declares that fail-closed behavior 'under enforce mode' is a success criterion that must be verified as part of closing the epic — implying enforce mode must be live for SC2 to be checkable. This creates a sequencing contradiction: if all stories before Story 7 are in warn mode, SC2 cannot be verified until Story 7 lands, but Story 7 requires ALL SCs to be verified first. Additionally, the SEQUENCING note says 'story 8 (UX) is independent and may land any time' but SC6 ('flow preserved + documented') lists UX correctness fixes as a success criterion that gates epic closure, yet Story 8 has no declared dependency. If Story 8 slips, SC6 would be unmet while the epic is otherwise closed.
findings.suggested_edit: Clarify whether SC2 is verifiable in a test environment (non-live) before the enforce-flip, or whether it is only fully verified after Story 7. If the former, add a sentence saying so. For Story 8 / SC6, either add Story 8 as a dependency of epic closure or split SC6 so the UX component is explicitly gated on Story 8.

findings.location: SC1–SC5 reference experiment/validation labels (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8); DELIVERY PRINCIPLES: 'Live-verify each story on real GitHub … AND by the named E-series/V-series experiments'
findings.finding: The plan's testing strategy is entirely encoded in experiment/validation labels (E2, E13, V0.3a-post-fix, etc.) that are defined in the external source-of-truth documents (option-a-pivot-plan.md, ADR-0020) and not inlined. An executor or reviewer cannot assess whether: (a) RED-before-GREEN ordering is enforced (i.e., do the experiments deliberately introduce a failing state and confirm it is red before fixing?), (b) boundary cases such as the 'empty diff', 'single-commit allowlisted PR', and 'token-expiry mid-run' are each covered by a named experiment, or (c) flakiness guards exist for ledger warm-hits (SC4). The plan mentions 'no flaky-check team block' as a goal but names no test that would catch a flaky warm-hit. Cannot confirm coverage without the external documents.
findings.suggested_edit: Inline a one-line description of each experiment/validation label at its point of reference (e.g., 'E13: a PR with allowlisted-only changes that carries un-reviewed content is confirmed BLOCKED'). Add an explicit RED-first note for Story 7 (enforce-flip), and name a specific test for the flakiness scenario in SC4.

findings.location: SC1–SC5, DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub'), NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT
findings.finding: The plan repeatedly references specific existing artefacts and behaviours without stating whether they already exist or need to be created: (1) 'required-checks.txt' — assumed to already track the live ruleset state; (2) 'review-coverage-invariant.yml', 'dangling-references.yml', 'ruleset-invariants.yml' — assumed to exist as CI wrappers with a well-defined 'enforce mode' toggle; (3) 'review-gate' check — assumed to be definable as a single always-runs required check but no confirmation that GitHub Rulesets support this naming/binding without additional branch-protection config; (4) 'merge-pipeline-checks' — referenced as the thing being swapped out, but its current implementation details (what it checks, how it exits) are unverified; (5) The 'R8 live-parity guard' and the 'R8 RED is repo-wide during live<->file skew window' language assumes R8 is an existing runbook or check whose behaviour is known; (6) 'coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs' (SC4) presumes a caching mechanism exists. An executor cannot proceed safely without verifying these artefacts exist, their current exit-code contracts, and how their 'enforce mode' is activated.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a pre-story 0 (or an explicit 'prerequisites' section) that lists every assumed-existing artefact with its file path and expected interface, and requires the executor to verify each against the actual repo before story 1 begins.

findings.location: SC1 ('Verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED'), SC2 ('a scope-stripped/expired token makes the gate RED … in ALL THREE carriers'), SC3 ('the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap is reflected on … the live ruleset'), SC5 ('Verified: a docs-only PR now requires review'), DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub (admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads)')
findings.finding: Multiple success criteria are explicitly tied to live GitHub operations that cannot be completed within a single agent working session without persistent real GitHub state: (a) SC1/SC5 require firing actual PR2 traffic and observing GitHub's merge-block UI; (b) SC2 requires a real expired/scope-stripped PAT to be presented to running CI — this cannot be simulated locally; (c) SC3 requires writing to the live GitHub ruleset and reading it back to confirm parity; (d) SC4's 'no flaky-check team block' can only be proven over multiple real CI runs across time. The plan provides no simulation/stub path — it explicitly says 'Live-verify … on real GitHub'. None of these are measurable inside a bounded session with only file-system and scripted test access.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC, define an offline/simulation acceptance path alongside the live-verification path: e.g., SC1 can be partially verified by a script that parses required-checks.txt and asserts 'review-gate' is present; SC2 can be verified by a unit test that stubs the GitHub token env-var to empty and asserts exit-code != 0; SC3 can be verified by a dry-run API call or a snapshot comparison. Mark live-GitHub steps as 'post-merge smoke' rather than blocking AC.

findings.location: SC1 ('a single always-runs required check `review-gate`'), SC3 ('retains `llm-review`; the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap'), SC5 ('removed from the review-gate allowlist … genuinely-inert artifacts … remain exempt')
findings.finding: The plan introduces at least three distinct required-check identifiers ('review-gate', 'llm-review', 'merge-pipeline-checks') plus three separate CI YAML wrappers (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml) all implementing overlapping fail-closed guard logic. Without seeing the existing CI surface it is impossible to confirm whether this is justified complexity or whether one well-configured check could subsume the others. The allowlist mechanism (SC4/SC5) also implies a bespoke exemption-registry layer whose scope is unspecified. If any of these already perform overlapping work, the plan is adding configuration surface without reducing it.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an explicit consolidation rationale in the plan (or the ADR reference) explaining why each of the three YAML wrappers must remain separate rather than being merged into a single composite check, and state what distinct contract each one owns.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT, SEQUENCING, SC3, SC4, SC5, DELIVERY PRINCIPLES
findings.finding: Several design decisions are left implicit or unresolved, forcing the executor to make judgment calls: (1) The plan says 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start' but does not name or describe stories 1-8 anywhere in the ticket — the executor must either infer them from the referenced external docs (option-a-pivot-plan.md, ADR-0020) or invent them. The plan body contains no story decomposition at all. (2) 'warn/advisory mode' vs 'enforce mode' toggle is mentioned repeatedly (SC2, DELIVERY PRINCIPLES) but how the toggle is implemented (env-var? file flag? CI input?) is never stated. (3) SC4 says 'commit whose diff is entirely within the event-sourced ticket store is exempt' — the boundary of 'event-sourced ticket store' is not defined (path glob? label?). (4) 'land the swap with no other PR2 in flight' (SC3 runbook) — the procedure for verifying this pre-condition is unspecified. (5) 'required-checks.txt' is referenced as both a source-of-truth file and as something that must match the live ruleset, but how it is kept in sync and who owns it is not stated.
findings.suggested_edit: Inline a minimal story list (even one sentence per story) directly in the ticket rather than deferring to external docs. Define the warn/enforce toggle mechanism concretely (e.g., 'ENFORCE=true env-var passed by the calling workflow'). Define 'event-sourced ticket store' as an explicit path pattern (e.g., 'data/tickets/**'). Add a one-liner runbook for the swap pre-condition check.

findings.location: SEQUENCING ('stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel') vs SC3/SC5 content; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Warn-first') vs SC2 ('fail-closed … in ALL THREE carriers'); SC4 ('coverage ledger warm-hits … no flaky-check team block') vs SC1 ('no code_changed skip')
findings.finding: Three internal tensions exist: (1) SEQUENCING declares stories 1-4 parallelisable, but story 5 depends on 1+2+4 and story 6 on 5. Since the stories are not described in the plan body, it is impossible to verify that 1-4 truly have no cross-story data dependencies — the sequencing claim is unverifiable against the plan itself. (2) DELIVERY PRINCIPLES say 'every story ships its enforcing logic in warn/advisory mode … BEFORE any enforce-flip', yet SC2 requires the gate to 'fail-closed on precondition/token failure under enforce mode' — it is not stated whether SC2 is verified only after the enforce-flip (story 7) or also in warn mode; if also in warn mode the 'warn-first' principle is undermined. (3) SC4 says the coverage ledger 'warm-hits across re-runs (no flaky-check team block)' implying caching, while SC1 says the gate 'runs on every base==main PR (no code_changed skip)' — caching and 'no skip' are in tension if the cache serves as a skip path.
findings.suggested_edit: Clarify (a) which SCs apply in warn mode vs enforce mode; (b) define 'warm-hit' to mean cache of per-SHA review results (not a skip of the check itself) so it does not conflict with SC1's 'no skip'; (c) inline the story list to make the parallel-dependency claim checkable.

findings.location: SC1–SC5 (all reference named experiments E2/E5/E6'/E8/E11/E13/E14/E15/V0.3a/V0.11/V0.12), DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('named E-series/V-series experiments')
findings.finding: The plan references a rich suite of named experiments (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, V0.12, R8) as the verification mechanism for every success criterion. However the experiment definitions — what stimulus each applies, what assertion it makes, what pass/fail looks like — exist only in the external document (docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md) which is not reproduced here. An executor relying only on this ticket cannot determine whether the test suite covers: failure-path (token expiry, scope strip), boundary cases (zero-diff PR, partial-SHA coverage), RED-before-GREEN ordering for the enforce-flip, or regression of the 'no false wedge' case. The testing plan is effectively outsourced to an unversioned external doc. If that doc drifts or is unavailable the test plan collapses.
findings.suggested_edit: Inline a minimal test matrix in the ticket (one row per SC: scenario | stimulus | expected exit/state | experiment ref). This does not need to replace the external docs but makes the testing plan self-contained and auditable without fetching option-a-pivot-plan.md.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1-SC6, DELIVERY PRINCIPLES 'Live-verify each story', named experiments E2/E5/E6'/E8/E11/E13/E14/E15/V0.3a/V0.11/R8
findings.finding: The plan references a rich set of named experiments and verification checkpoints (E-series, V-series, R8) as its test plan, but these are defined entirely in external documents (docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md, docs/adr/0020). The plan text itself does not reproduce or summarize what each experiment actually does, what its pass/fail observable is, or how RED-before-GREEN is demonstrated. For example, SC1 references E2/E13 and SC2 references V0.3a-post-fix/R8 fail-closed, but a reader of this ticket alone cannot determine: (a) what stimulus triggers E13, (b) what the concrete assertion for 'V0.3a-post-fix' is, or (c) whether RED-before-GREEN ordering is explicitly required and verified before each enforce-flip. The plan also does not describe boundary cases such as: partial-SHA coverage (some SHAs reviewed, some not), concurrent PRs during the live<->file skew window, or re-run determinism (warm-hit vs. cold-start). The warn-first principle partially covers failure-path sequencing but does not enumerate the specific failure modes being tested in CI.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC entry, add a one-line 'Verified by: <what observable is checked, against which fixture>' inline — e.g., SC1: 'A PR2 carrying SHA X (not in ledger) is opened; review-gate check must be RED; after ledger update it must turn GREEN.' This keeps the external docs as source of truth while making the test intent self-contained in the ticket.

findings.location: SC1 'single always-runs required check review-gate'; SC2 'ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'; SC3 'live ruleset requires review-gate AND retains llm-review'
findings.finding: The plan introduces at least four distinct CI/ruleset artifacts (review-gate check, review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml) plus a coverage ledger, a required-checks.txt file, and a live ruleset — all cooperating to enforce a single gate. Without access to the existing codebase it cannot be confirmed whether some of these already exist and are being reused vs. newly created. If dangling-references.yml and ruleset-invariants.yml are already present for other purposes, adding fail-closed behavior to them for this gate may be appropriate reuse; if they are new, three separate YAML files doing broadly similar 'check and fail-closed on precondition' logic raises a Rule-of-Three concern that a single parameterised wrapper could serve all three. Additionally, maintaining both a required-checks.txt flat file AND a live ruleset as authoritative representations of the same state (SC3: 'reflected on BOTH') is a dual-source-of-truth smell that risks permanent drift. The R8 live-parity guard is the mitigation, but the design choice itself is not justified in the plan.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a sentence in the plan explaining why required-checks.txt and the live ruleset must both be maintained rather than treating the live ruleset as the sole source of truth. If the three YAML carriers are new, consider whether a single parameterised reusable workflow covers all three cases; if they pre-exist, note that explicitly.

findings.location: Title, NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT, SUCCESS CRITERIA, DELIVERY PRINCIPLES

Multiple sections: 'Source of truth: docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md'; SC1-SC6 referencing E2/E5/E6'/E8/E11/E13/E14/E15/V0.3a-post-fix/V0.11/R8; SEQUENCING 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 depends on 1-6'
findings.finding: The plan references eight distinct stories (1-8) and their dependency graph but provides zero description of what any individual story's scope, deliverable, or acceptance criterion is. An executing agent cannot proceed to story 1 without reading the external 'option-a-pivot-plan.md'. Similarly, all named experiments (E2, E5, E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8) are opaque without that external document — making this ticket non-self-contained and requiring the agent to stop and resolve external references before acting. The COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER mentioned in the delivery principles is never instantiated for any story. The phrase 'Do NOT use /dso:sprint' is a negative constraint with no positive replacement workflow named.
findings.suggested_edit: Expand the SEQUENCING section with a short stub for each story (1-8) naming at minimum: what file/check/config it touches, its single deliverable, and its COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER observable. Inline the definition of each E/V/R experiment reference either here or in a collapsible appendix, so the ticket is executable without opening the external handoff doc.

findings.location: SEQUENCING: 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel … story 7 (enforce-flip) depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 (UX) is independent'; SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1-SC6 covering gate logic, CI wrappers, live ruleset, coverage ledger, allowlist policy, UX fixes, documentation, ADR update
findings.finding: This is described as an epic but the ticket contains no child story definitions — only a dependency graph of numbered story slots. Six distinct success criteria each span different system layers (GitHub ruleset API, CI YAML, coverage ledger, allowlist policy, UX/PR titles, documentation). The plan acknowledges stories 1-8 exist but does not decompose them here, meaning the ticket as written is simultaneously an epic (too large) and not an epic (no children are linked or described). An agent treating this as a single executable unit would need to implement all eight stories sequentially in one session, which is not coherent. Story 7 (the enforce-flip) is explicitly identified as 'the only hard-to-reverse step' and 'hard-gated' — it should be a standalone child ticket with its own AC, runbook reference, and rollback procedure rather than a bullet in a dependency list.
findings.suggested_edit: Convert this ticket explicitly to an epic/parent with 8 linked child tickets (one per story slot). At minimum, stub out each child with: title, one-paragraph scope, key deliverable, dependencies, and COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER. Story 7 in particular should be a separate ticket with the runbook inlined and a mandatory sign-off checkpoint before execution.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1-SC6 vs. plan body; SEQUENCING; 'story 8 (UX) is independent'
findings.finding: Three coherence gaps: (1) SC6 references 'UX correctness fixes (descriptive PR1 titles; sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle)' and 'story 8 (UX)' but no corresponding description of what these UX changes entail appears in the plan body — SC6 is an orphan criterion with no described implementation work. (2) The plan body mentions a 'completion-verifier PASS' as part of the epic-close condition but this verifier is never described — what it is, who runs it, what it checks. (3) The NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT paragraph introduces the concept of 'per-SHA coverage' which is central to SC1, but the coverage ledger mechanism (how SHAs are recorded, queried, and persisted) is described nowhere in the plan body — SC1's criterion cannot be verified against the plan because the implementation approach is missing. Terminology is mostly consistent (review-gate, fail-closed, warn/enforce modes are used uniformly), though 'merge-pipeline-checks' (SC3) appears only once and is not defined or linked to a prior concept.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Plan body' section that, for SC6, describes the UX changes (what changes to PR1 titles, what the draft-umbrella lifecycle change is); for the completion-verifier, names the script/check and its assertions; and for the coverage ledger (SC1/SC4), describes the storage mechanism (file path, format, update trigger). Define 'merge-pipeline-checks' on first use or replace with the term used elsewhere.

findings.location: SEQUENCING section vs. SC3/SC6 and story dependency statements
findings.finding: The plan says 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 is independent.' However SC3 references an R8 live-parity guard that 'fail-closes under enforce' — R8 is described elsewhere as a repo-wide RED window during the live<->file skew window, implying it is part of story 7 (the enforce-flip). If R8 is also a prerequisite artefact for SC2's 'R8 fail-closed' verification, then SC2 cannot be closed before story 7, yet SC2 is expected to be verified before story 7's hard gate is satisfied. This creates a potential circular dependency: SC2 references R8 fail-closed, R8 is the enforce-flip story (7), but story 7 depends on SC2 already being verified. The plan text alone cannot resolve whether 'R8' is a check name, a test label, or a phase label. Cannot confirm without the referenced source-of-truth docs.
findings.suggested_edit: Explicitly disambiguate 'R8' — state whether it is a CI check name, a test-run label, or a phase label — and add a cross-reference clarifying at which story it is introduced vs. where it is first required for verification.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1, SC2, SC3, SC4, SC5 — all contain 'Verified live' / 'Verified:' clauses and reference E-series/V-series experiments
findings.finding: Multiple acceptance criteria require out-of-session, live GitHub validation that an automated agent cannot perform in a single working session: (1) SC1 requires 'an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED' — this requires a real GitHub PR to be created, the ruleset to be live, and a human or privileged PAT to observe the block. (2) SC2 requires verifying that a scope-stripped/expired token makes the gate RED in three live CI carriers. (3) SC3 requires confirming a live ruleset and observing drift detection. (4) SC4 references 'live PR2 traffic'. None of these can be completed purely through code changes and unit/integration tests within a single agent session. The referenced experiments (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11) are named but not defined in this plan, making their pass/fail criteria unmeasurable without the external source-of-truth document.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC, add a locally-executable proxy verification step (e.g., a dry-run script, a mock GitHub API response, a test fixture simulating an expired token) that an agent can run in-session. Separate the 'live GitHub' verification into an explicit manual sign-off step that is clearly outside the agent's execution scope, and mark those SCs accordingly.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT and SC2: 'enforcing checks must fail-closed … in ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'
findings.finding: The plan asserts these three specific YAML workflow files exist and already implement (or can be patched to implement) fail-closed behaviour on token/precondition failure. This assumes: (a) these files exist at the named paths in the repo, (b) they each have a single identifiable token-consumption step that can be wrapped, and (c) the 'enforce mode' flag mechanism is already present or straightforwardly addable. None of these premises are verified in the plan text. If any file is named differently, structured differently, or does not yet exist, the story scoping is wrong.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a prerequisite step: 'Confirm that review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, and ruleset-invariants.yml exist at the expected paths and identify the exact step in each that consumes the LLM/PAT token.' Reference the actual repo paths explicitly.

findings.location: DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Warn-first') and story 7 ('enforce-flip') dependency statement; SUCCESS CRITERIA section as a whole
findings.finding: The plan defines a clear end-state (SC1-SC6 all met + 'completion-verifier PASS') but does not define what the 'completion-verifier' is, how it is run, or what its pass condition looks like. This is the sole named termination signal for the epic and it is a black box. Additionally, the 'warn-first → enforce-flip' path describes story 7 as 'the only hard-to-reverse step' and requires 'land the swap with no other PR2 in flight,' but provides no mechanism in the plan for an agent to confirm this precondition (no tooling, no check, no script). The runbook is referenced but not included, so termination for story 7 is unverifiable from plan text alone.
findings.suggested_edit: Define the 'completion-verifier' explicitly: name the script/command, its inputs, and its PASS output. For the story-7 precondition ('no other PR2 in flight'), add a concrete check command (e.g., a GitHub API query) that an executor can run to confirm the precondition before proceeding.

findings.location: SC3: 'required-checks.txt and the live ruleset'; SC5: 'docs/**, skills, behavior-driving config … removed from the review-gate allowlist'
findings.finding: The plan names specific artefacts — 'required-checks.txt', 'review-gate allowlist', 'review-coverage-invariant.yml', 'dangling-references.yml', 'ruleset-invariants.yml', 'docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md', 'docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md' — but does not enumerate the full set of files that will be created, modified, or deleted. In particular: (a) the allowlist file(s) for the review-gate are not named; (b) the 'skills' and 'behavior-driving config' surfaces referenced in SC5 are not identified by path; (c) it is unclear whether 'required-checks.txt' is a repo file or a live GitHub ruleset export, and which tooling reads it. Blast radius is not explicitly acknowledged. Cannot confirm these paths exist or are complete without codebase access.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Files affected' table listing each file to be created/modified/deleted (path, change type). At minimum name the allowlist file(s), the 'skills' directory or config file paths, and clarify whether required-checks.txt is a committed file or a generated snapshot.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT and opening paragraph
SEQUENCING section + individual story dependency text
findings.finding: The plan references 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 is independent' but the individual story bodies are not reproduced in the ticket text, so their stated dependencies cannot be cross-checked for coherence. There is also a potential tension between the 'warn-first' delivery principle (every story ships in warn/advisory mode first) and SC2, which requires fail-closed enforcement 'under enforce mode' — it is unclear at which story boundary warn mode transitions to enforce mode, and whether the sequencing guarantees SC2 is verified before story 7 triggers. On a benign reading, story 7 is the enforce-flip and SC2 is verified in warn mode; but the plan does not state this explicitly, leaving a gap.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a one-sentence note to the SEQUENCING section clarifying: 'SC2 is verified in warn mode (stories 1–6) so that enforce mode behaviour is confirmed before story 7 flips the gate. The enforce-flip in story 7 is the only step that moves checks from advisory to hard-fail.'

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA (SC1–SC6) and DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub')
findings.finding: Multiple success criteria are only verifiable through live GitHub operations that cannot be simulated or completed within a single automated agent session: SC1 requires 'verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED'; SC2 requires 'a scope-stripped/expired token makes the gate RED … in ALL THREE carriers'; SC3 requires confirming that a live main ruleset retains two named required checks; SC4 requires observing 'no flaky-check team block' on live PR2 traffic; SC5 requires observing 'a docs-only PR now requires review' on live traffic. The delivery principle 'Live-verify each story on real GitHub (admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads)' further cements that out-of-session, real-repository validation is the intended verification mechanism. No simulation or mock substitute is described. An agent cannot complete or confirm these criteria within one session.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC, add a 'local verifiable proxy' sub-criterion that can be checked without live GitHub: e.g. for SC1, run the review-gate script locally against a fixture SHA list and assert exit-code 1; for SC2, inject a dummy token and assert non-zero exit; for SC3, diff required-checks.txt against an expected value. Label the live-traffic checks as 'post-deploy smoke tests' rather than acceptance criteria the agent must pass.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; SC2 ('ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'); SC3 ('live main ruleset requires review-gate and retains llm-review')
findings.finding: The plan asserts without verification: (a) that the three named workflow files (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml) already exist and already carry the 'CI wrappers' that need fail-closed hardening; (b) that a live main ruleset already exists with 'llm-review' as a required check; (c) that a check named 'merge-pipeline-checks' currently exists in the required-checks list and will be swapped for 'review-gate'. These are architectural premises the agent must confirm against the actual repository before building on them. If any is absent or named differently, the implementation steps break.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Prerequisite verification' step at the top of each story: list the file paths and ruleset names to confirm exist before coding begins, and explicitly name the fallback if they are absent (e.g. 'create the file from scratch if not present').
