findings.suggested_edit: For each SC, add a back-reference to the story/step that implements it. Define 'merge-pipeline-checks', 'llm-review', 'event-sourced ticket store', and 'completion-verifier' in a Glossary or Definitions section to lock terminology.

findings.location: SC2: 'ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'; SC1 'review-gate' check + existing 'llm-review' check
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new 'review-gate' required check in addition to the existing 'llm-review' check (SC3), and enforces fail-closed behavior across three separate workflow files. Without codebase access it is unclear whether a single unified wrapper or a small shared composite action could replace per-file duplication. If fail-closed token logic is repeated in all three YAMLs, that is a Rule-of-Three violation. Cannot confirm without inspecting the three workflow files.
findings.suggested_edit: If the fail-closed token-check logic is duplicated across the three YAMLs, extract it into a shared composite action (e.g. .github/actions/fail-closed-token-check) called by each workflow.

findings.location: SEQUENCING paragraph; SC2 'V0.3a-post-fix, R8 fail-closed'; 'completion-verifier PASS'; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES 'named E-series/V-series experiments'
findings.finding: Several terms and references are unresolved placeholders an executor would need to stop and look up: (1) E2/E5/E6'/E8/E11/E13/E14/E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8 — these experiment/run identifiers are cited as verification evidence but are not defined in the plan. An executor cannot know what action to take. (2) 'completion-verifier' is named but not defined. (3) 'live<->file skew window' in the reversibility note is not explained. (4) 'per-SHA coverage' (SC1) is not linked to a concrete data structure or script. (5) Story content is absent — only story numbers and dependencies are listed, leaving the executor without any description of what to build in each story.
findings.suggested_edit: Either inline the E/V/R experiment definitions (inputs, expected outputs, pass criteria) or link to a single canonical reference that is included in the ticket. Add a 'Stories' section with at minimum a one-sentence 'what to build' per story. Define 'completion-verifier' with its invocation command.

findings.location: DELIVERY PRINCIPLES 'Warn-first'; SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC4; E-series/V-series experiment references
findings.finding: The plan references a large set of named experiments (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8) as the testing strategy, but none of their test designs, inputs, expected outputs, or RED-before-GREEN ordering are described in this plan. It is impossible to assess testing completeness without access to the external experiment documents. Fail-path coverage is mentioned conceptually (SC2: 'scope-stripped/expired token') but no test script, fixture, or stub is described. Boundary cases (e.g., empty diff, partial SHA coverage, zero-commit PR) are not named.
findings.suggested_edit: Inline the minimum viable test matrix: (a) happy path: clean reviewed PR passes; (b) fail-closed: stub token returns 401 → gate exits non-zero; (c) exemption: ticket-store-only diff skips; (d) partial coverage: one unreviewed SHA blocks. Reference the experiment IDs as labels on these cases rather than as the entire test plan.

findings.location: SEQUENCING vs. DELIVERY PRINCIPLES; SC3 vs. SC1
findings.finding: 1. DELIVERY PRINCIPLES states 'every story ships its enforcing logic in warn/advisory mode … BEFORE any enforce-flip' but the SEQUENCING section does not identify which story performs the warn→enforce flip per workflow file — only story 7 is called 'enforce-flip'. If stories 1–4 ship enforcement logic (as SC2 implies for the three YAMLs), there is ambiguity about whether each story ships in warn mode first or directly in enforce mode. 2. SC1 says 'a single always-runs required check review-gate' while SC3 says 'live main ruleset requires review-gate AND retains llm-review' — two required checks, not one. This is not necessarily a conflict (llm-review may be a sub-check feeding review-gate) but the relationship is not explained anywhere in the plan, creating ambiguity for the executor about the final required-check count. 3. The REVERSIBILITY note mentions 'R8 RED is repo-wide during the live<->file skew window' but the SEQUENCING section does not call out any story that manages this freeze window, leaving a gap.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a sentence clarifying the relationship between 'review-gate' and 'llm-review' (e.g., 'llm-review is a sub-job within the review-gate workflow'). Add an explicit story or step for managing the live<->file skew freeze window. Clarify which stories deliver in warn mode and which flip to enforce.

findings.location: SC2: 'review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'; SC3: 'required-checks.txt'; SC5: 'review-gate allowlist'
findings.finding: The plan names four specific files (three YAMLs + required-checks.txt) and an allowlist artifact as edit targets. Without codebase access it is impossible to confirm these files exist, are at the named paths, and that no additional callers/consumers reference them. The blast radius of adding a new required check ('review-gate') to the live ruleset is acknowledged (R8 freeze window) but the plan does not list any downstream automation (e.g., branch protection rules, merge queues, status-check dashboards) that might also need updating.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Files touched' checklist and explicitly note any other automation (merge queue config, status-check dashboards, branch-protection YAML if managed as code) that references the existing required checks.

findings.location: SEQUENCING: 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel … story 5 … story 6 … story 7 … story 8'
findings.finding: The plan describes an 8-story epic but provides no story-level content — no description of what each story builds, its acceptance criteria, its test, or its estimated size. As written this is an epic plan, not a leaf-ticket plan. An agent executing this ticket would have no story-level guidance and would need to decompose the work itself, which is a real design decision being deferred to the executor. The sequencing graph (1-4 parallel, 5→1+2+4, 6→5, 7→1-6, 8→independent) is present but the actual content of each story node is absent.
findings.suggested_edit: Either (a) add at minimum a one-paragraph 'what to build + done-when' for each of the 8 stories, or (b) convert this ticket into a pure epic/parent and create 8 child tickets, each with its own full plan. Option (b) is preferred for a 'warn-first then enforce-flip' multi-phase delivery.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; SC1 'per-SHA coverage'; SC3 'required-checks.txt … live ruleset'; SC4 'event-sourced ticket store'; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES 'admin PAT: provision/read'
findings.finding: The plan rests on several unverified environmental premises: (1) A live GitHub ruleset for 'main' already exists and is manageable via the provided admin PAT — not confirmed. (2) 'required-checks.txt' already exists as a source-of-truth file — not confirmed. (3) The 'event-sourced ticket store' is an existing artifact with a defined diff boundary — not confirmed. (4) 'per-SHA coverage' implies an existing coverage ledger/database — its schema, location, and warm-cache mechanism are assumed to exist (SC4 'coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs') but not defined. (5) The three named YAMLs exist at their implied paths. All five are assumptions that must be verified before implementation begins.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Prerequisites / verified assumptions' section that explicitly confirms: (a) ruleset ID and admin PAT scope, (b) path and schema of required-checks.txt, (c) definition and path of the ticket store, (d) schema of the coverage ledger, (e) paths of the three workflow YAMLs. Mark each as 'confirmed' or 'to be confirmed in story 0'.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC6; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES 'Live-verify each story on real GitHub'
findings.finding: Multiple acceptance criteria require live GitHub state validation that cannot be completed within a single automated agent session. SC1 requires a real PR2 to be BLOCKED live ('an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED'); SC2 requires a scope-stripped/expired token to produce a RED gate on a real CI run; SC3 requires observing a live ruleset retain required checks; SC4/SC5 require live PR runs to confirm no-wedge and docs-only routing. These are inherently out-of-session, real-infrastructure validations ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub') with no simulation fallback specified. No acceptance criterion can be declared PASS by static analysis or unit test alone — the plan provides no mock/stub/replay substitute.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC, add a parallel 'offline verifiable' sub-criterion: e.g., SC1 → 'a test harness simulating the ruleset decision tree with a mocked GitHub Checks API returns BLOCKED for the un-reviewed-content case'; SC2 → 'a unit test that injects a zeroed token into each CI wrapper asserts exit-nonzero'. Keep the live-verify as a final gate but make it a sign-off step, not the only measure.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT paragraph; SUCCESS CRITERIA preamble
Title vs. plan body
SEQUENCING paragraph; SUCCESS CRITERIA 'epic closes only when ALL are met + completion-verifier PASS'
findings.finding: The plan references a 'completion-verifier PASS' as a mandatory gate but never defines what the completion-verifier is, what it checks, or how to invoke it. Similarly, stories 1–8 are referenced ('story 5 depends on 1+2+4', etc.) but no story definitions appear in the plan text — only the epic shell. This makes it impossible to verify that the union of described steps actually reaches all six success criteria, since the steps themselves are not present. An agent executing this epic-level plan cannot determine whether an intermediate story is 'verified' without those definitions.
findings.suggested_edit: Embed (or link to a readable artifact) the per-story acceptance criteria and the completion-verifier script/checklist. At minimum, define the completion-verifier inline as a script or named CI job.

findings.location: SEQUENCING paragraph vs. SUCCESS CRITERIA; SC6 vs. plan body
findings.finding: 1) Stories 1–8 are cited as the decomposition vehicle throughout (SEQUENCING, DELIVERY PRINCIPLES), but the plan body contains no story definitions — every criterion that says 'see each story's…' points to content that does not exist in this plan. The criteria are therefore orphaned from their driving descriptions. 2) SC6 includes 'sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle' and 'descriptive PR1 titles' (UX story 8) but no part of the plan body describes how those UX fixes will be implemented. 3) Terminology: 'review-gate' and 'llm-review' are used as both check names and conceptual roles interchangeably — e.g., SC3 says 'requires review-gate and retains llm-review' without clarifying whether these are the same check at different lifecycle stages or two permanently co-existing checks.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a terminology glossary distinguishing 'llm-review' (the review job) from 'review-gate' (the required-check name). For SC6, add a plan-body description of the UX fix scope. Link or embed story text so no AC is orphaned.

findings.location: SC2 — 'ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'; SC3 — 'R8 live-parity guard'
findings.finding: The plan introduces (or extends) at least four named CI workflow files plus a 'coverage ledger' (SC4 'warm-hits across re-runs'), a 'completion-verifier', and a 'runbook' for the enforce-flip. It is not possible from plan text alone to verify whether the coverage-ledger warm-cache mechanism is a new bespoke artifact or reuses an existing caching primitive (GitHub Actions cache). If it is a new one-off system, that would be over-engineering. Similarly, having fail-closed logic duplicated across three separate carrier workflows rather than centralised in one composite action may be unnecessary proliferation — but this depends on whether a shared composite action already exists.
findings.suggested_edit: Clarify whether the fail-closed token-check logic is extracted into a single reusable composite action that all three carriers call, vs. copy-pasted. If it is the latter, flag it as a known trade-off.

findings.location: SEQUENCING paragraph; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES 'Warn-first'; SUCCESS CRITERIA 'epic closes only when ALL are met'
findings.finding: The plan defers multiple concrete design decisions to the per-story level without providing those stories. An executing agent encounters: (a) 'each story names a COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER' — but no triggers are defined here; (b) 'the enforce-flip story carries the runbook' — runbook content unspecified; (c) 'land the swap with no other PR2 in flight' — the mechanism to detect and enforce this constraint is never described; (d) 'provision/read' PAT vs. 'containment reads' non-admin PAT — no spec for which token is used in which workflow step. Each of these is a real design decision that an agent would have to invent rather than execute.
findings.suggested_edit: For each deferred item, either inline the decision (e.g., 'detect no other open PR2 by running `gh pr list --base main --state open --json number | jq length == 1`') or explicitly mark it as a prerequisite input that must be provided before execution starts.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC5; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES 'Live-verify each story… by the named E-series/V-series experiments'
findings.finding: The plan references a comprehensive experiment suite (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a, V0.11, V0.12, R8) as the test plan, but the content of these experiments is not included in the plan text — they presumably live in docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md. An agent cannot verify RED-before-GREEN ordering, failure-path coverage, or boundary cases without reading that document. The plan does not state whether any of these experiments are automated (runnable scripts) or manual observation protocols. Structural/boundary tests for the fail-closed paths (token expiry, precondition failure, SHA gap) are mentioned only at the SC level, not as explicit test steps.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a testing section that, for each of the three fail-closed paths (expired token, precondition failure, un-reviewed SHA), names the exact command to run and the expected exit code/output to assert. Reference the experiment IDs as supplementary, not as the primary test spec.

findings.location: SEQUENCING vs. SC3; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES 'warn-first' vs. SC2
findings.finding: 1) SEQUENCING states 'story 7 (enforce-flip) depends on 1-6 ALL verified', but SC3 says 'the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap is reflected on BOTH required-checks.txt and the live ruleset' — this swap sounds like it is part of story 7, yet SC3 is listed as a success criterion the epic must satisfy without any gating dependency. If SC3 is only satisfied at story 7, then stories 1–6 cannot individually close with all SCs met, creating a sequencing contradiction. 2) DELIVERY PRINCIPLES say all stories ship in 'warn/advisory mode' first, but SC2 explicitly validates 'under enforce mode' behavior — implying enforce-mode testing must happen before story 7 (the enforce-flip), yet the warn-first principle says enforce mode should not be active until story 7. This creates a contradiction between the testing requirement (SC2 validated pre-flip) and the delivery principle (no enforce mode before story 7).
findings.suggested_edit: Clarify whether SC3 is an epic-level criterion satisfied only at story 7 completion or a per-story invariant. For SC2, specify that the enforce-mode fail-closed validation is done in a staging/test environment or via a feature-flagged dry-run, not against the live main ruleset, to resolve the warn-first contradiction.

findings.location: SC2 — 'review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'; SC3 — 'required-checks.txt'; SC5 — 'review-gate allowlist'
findings.finding: The plan names five specific files that will be modified (three YML workflows, required-checks.txt, and the allowlist) but does not name the allowlist file, the coverage-ledger file/format, the completion-verifier script path, or the runbook document path. The 'merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap' in SC3 implies renaming or retiring a check but the old check name 'merge-pipeline-checks' is used only once with no description of where it is defined. Whether callers/consumers of that check name (e.g., branch protection rules, other workflow `needs:` arrays) are also updated is unacknowledged.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a file-change manifest listing every file to be created/modified, including the allowlist path, coverage-ledger path, and the location of every reference to 'merge-pipeline-checks' that must be updated when it is renamed.

findings.location: SEQUENCING paragraph — 'stories 1–8'; overall plan structure
findings.finding: This is correctly labeled an epic, and it references 8 child stories with named inter-dependencies (story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 depends on 1–6; story 8 is independent). However, none of those child stories are defined or linked within the plan text. The epic therefore cannot be executed as written — it is a decomposition reference without decomposition content. The overall scope (ruleset configuration, three CI workflow changes, coverage ledger, allowlist restructure, PAT provisioning, runbook, ADR update, UX fixes) spans at least 6 distinct subsystems, making this too large for a single agent session even if it were a leaf task.
findings.suggested_edit: Attach or link the 8 child story definitions as sub-tickets or appendices before assigning to an agent. Each child story should include its own description, acceptance criteria, file list, and course-correction trigger as promised by the DELIVERY PRINCIPLES.

findings.location: SC3 — 'live main ruleset requires review-gate and retains llm-review'; SC4 — 'coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs'; SC2 — 'ALL THREE carriers'
findings.finding: The plan assumes: (a) a 'main' ruleset already exists on the GitHub repo and is editable by the provisioned PAT — unverified; (b) the 'coverage ledger' warm-cache mechanism already exists and is functional ('warm-hits across re-runs') — unverified; (c) the three named carrier workflows already exist and contain token-check logic that merely needs hardening — unverified. If any of these are net-new artifacts rather than existing ones, the scope changes materially. The plan also assumes the 'event-sourced ticket store' exemption can be expressed as a path-glob pattern that the review-gate already understands — unverified.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a prerequisite-verification checklist at the top of the plan: 'Before story 1 begins, confirm: (1) live ruleset named X exists; (2) workflows review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml exist at paths Y; (3) coverage ledger cache key Z is populated.' Make these explicit preconditions, not implicit assumptions.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA (SC1–SC6) vs. plan body (DELIVERY PRINCIPLES, SEQUENCING, NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT)
findings.finding:
Several coherence and terminology gaps exist:

1. ORPHAN CRITERIA IN SC: SC6 mentions 'descriptive PR1 titles; sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle' and 'ADR-0020 updated with execution results' — neither of these is described anywhere in the plan body (DELIVERY PRINCIPLES, SEQUENCING, or the NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT section). They appear only in SC6, with no story/phase description giving implementation detail.

2. DESCRIBED WORK WITH NO CRITERION: The DELIVERY PRINCIPLES section describes a 'warn-first' pattern (every story ships in warn/advisory mode first, then an enforce-flip) and a 'COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER' per story — but no success criterion explicitly validates that warn-mode was actually exercised on live PR2 traffic before the enforce-flip (SC3 covers the enforce-flip result, not the warn-mode validation gate).

3. TERMINOLOGY INCONSISTENCY — 'review-gate' vs. 'llm-review': SC1 refers to a single required check named 'review-gate'; SC3 says the live ruleset 'retains llm-review'; the plan body's NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT says 'LLM review that is ruleset-enforced'. It is not made explicit in the plan body whether 'review-gate' replaces 'llm-review', runs alongside it, or is a new name for it. SC3 describes a 'merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap' but the body never defines what 'merge-pipeline-checks' is or what check it replaces.

4. TERMINOLOGY INCONSISTENCY — 'R8' usage: 'R8' is used in SC2 ('R8 fail-closed'), SC3 ('R8 live-parity guard'), and DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('R8 RED is repo-wide during the live<->file skew window') with three slightly different meanings (a red-state condition, a guard name, and a repo-wide lock). The plan body never formally defines R8.

5. NEAR-DUPLICATE REQUIREMENT: SC1 states 'fail-closes unless every SHA in origin/main..head is proven reviewed (per-SHA coverage)' and SC2 states 'all review-enforcing CI wrappers fail-closed on precondition/token failure'. These partially overlap on the concept of fail-closed behavior but target different failure modes (missing review coverage vs. bad token/precondition). This is a borderline duplicate but can be read as complementary — flagged as minor for clarity.

6. EXPERIMENT REFERENCES WITHOUT BODY DESCRIPTION: SC1 references [E2/E13], SC2 references [V0.3a-post-fix], SC4 references [E5/E8/E14/E15], SC5 references [E11/E-compute-hash], SC3 references [E6', V0.11]. These experiment IDs are cited only in criteria; the plan body does not describe what any of these experiments entail or what 'PASS' means for them. The source-of-truth document (option-a-pivot-plan.md) is referenced but not reproduced, leaving a gap for anyone reading only the ticket.
findings.suggested_edit:
1. Add a sentence in the plan body (under SC6 or a dedicated UX/docs section) describing the PR1 title fix, sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle work, and ADR-0020 update so SC6 is not an orphan.
2. Add a success criterion (or extend SC3) that explicitly requires: 'warn-mode was validated on live PR2 traffic (observed advisory-only failures) before the enforce-flip was executed.'
3. In the plan body, define R8 (e.g., 'R8: the live-parity invariant check in ruleset-invariants.yml') once and use that label consistently.
4. Add a one-line definition distinguishing 'review-gate' from 'llm-review' in the plan body, clarifying whether they coexist or one replaces the other.
5. Either inline a summary table of the E/V experiment definitions, or explicitly state 'experiment definitions are in docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md §X' so reviewers know where to find pass/fail criteria.

findings.location: Plan title vs. NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT, SUCCESS CRITERIA, DELIVERY PRINCIPLES, and SEQUENCING sections
Plan description, NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT, SUCCESS CRITERIA, SEQUENCING sections
Overall plan: title, NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT, SUCCESS CRITERIA, SEQUENCING
Multiple sections of the plan description and Success Criteria
findings.finding:
The plan references a large body of external documents and named artifacts as its source of truth — 'docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md (CONVERGED, review #1-6 + experiments V0.1-V0.12)', 'docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md', 'Phase 2 (§5)', named experiments (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, E-compute-hash), named experiment runs (V0.1-V0.12, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11), named checks (review-gate, llm-review, merge-pipeline-checks, review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml), and 'required-checks.txt' — but NONE of their concrete contents are reproduced or summarised in this plan. An executing agent cannot determine:

1. What specific GitHub ruleset configuration changes are required (which fields, which required-check names, which bypass actors).
2. What the 'two-tier flow' is, what 'PR1' and 'PR2' mean, or what the existing CI job topology looks like — all are assumed known from the missing external docs.
3. What 'warn mode' vs 'enforce mode' means concretely for each of the three named YML files (what env var / flag controls the mode flip?).
4. What the 'enforce-flip' story (story 7) actually does — the plan references it but provides no story-level detail anywhere in the ticket.
5. What 'stories 1-8' are — they are referenced in SEQUENCING and the SC section but their scopes/acceptance criteria are entirely absent.
6. What the 'coverage ledger' is, where it lives, and how 'warm-hits' should be implemented.
7. What 'per-SHA coverage' means operationally (hash of what? stored where? what constitutes 'proven reviewed'?).
8. What 'R8 RED' means and what the runbook for the live<->file skew window contains.
9. What 'allowlisted-only PR2' means — what the allowlist is, where it is configured, and what 'carrying un-reviewed content' means in a checkable sense.
10. What 'E-series/V-series experiments' the agent is supposed to run and how to run them — no experiment definitions are present.

Without the referenced external documents, an agent executing this ticket would be forced to stop and ask clarifying questions at virtually every implementation step. The plan is effectively a summary/index of a larger design that is not included.
findings.suggested_edit:
Inline or append the minimum-necessary content from the referenced external documents directly into this ticket:
- For each story (1-8): a one-paragraph scope statement plus its specific acceptance criteria and course-correction trigger.
- For the 'enforce-flip': the exact GitHub API / UI steps to add/swap required checks on the ruleset.
- For warn vs enforce mode: the exact mechanism (e.g., 'set ENV VAR ENFORCE_MODE=1 in the workflow; when absent the script exits 0 with a warning annotation').
- For per-SHA coverage: the storage mechanism (e.g., 'a JSON ledger committed to .github/review-ledger.json keyed by commit SHA').
- For the allowlist: the file path and current contents.
- For each named experiment (E2, E5, E6', etc.): a one-line description of what to run and what a PASS looks like.
This removes the dependency on files the agent may not be able to locate or interpret unambiguously.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA (SC1–SC6) vs. DELIVERY PRINCIPLES vs. SEQUENCING section
findings.finding:
Several coherence and terminology issues exist, though none are plan-blocking:

1. ORPHAN CRITERIA / DESCRIBED WORK WITHOUT A CRITERION:
   - The DELIVERY PRINCIPLES describe a specific 'COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER' for every story and a reversibility runbook ('land the swap with no other PR2 in flight — R8 RED is repo-wide during the live<->file skew window'), but no success criterion (SC1–SC6) verifies that course-correction triggers were defined or exercised. These are mentioned as mandatory story-level elements ('Each story names a COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER') but never appear in the epic's closing gate.
   - 'Warn-first' mode validation on 'live PR2 traffic BEFORE any enforce-flip' is described in DELIVERY PRINCIPLES but is not represented as a verifiable success criterion. SC3 captures the enforce-flip outcome but not the prerequisite that warn-mode was validated first.
   - The named experiments 'E-compute-hash' (referenced in SC5) and 'E13' (referenced in SC1) appear only in the SC references; neither is described or defined anywhere in the plan body, making it impossible to assess what observable they require.

2. TERMINOLOGY INCONSISTENCIES:
   - 'review-gate' (SC1, SC3) vs. 'merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap' (SC3): the term 'merge-pipeline-checks' is introduced once without prior definition. It is unclear whether this refers to a specific existing check name or a class of checks.
   - 'R8' appears in SC2 ('R8 fail-closed') and SC3 ('R8 live-parity guard', 'R8 RED') but is never defined in the plan body. It is presumably a review or experiment identifier from the source-of-truth doc, but a reader of this ticket alone cannot resolve it.
   - 'PR2' is used throughout ('live PR2 traffic', 'no other PR2 in flight') without being defined in the plan body. The two-tier flow presumably distinguishes PR1 and PR2, but the distinction is only inferable from context, not stated.
   - 'per-SHA coverage' (SC1) and 'coverage ledger' (SC4) refer to the same concept using different language; it is unclear whether these are the same artifact or different components.
   - 'origin/main..head' (SC1) uses a git-refspec style notation that is consistent, but no other part of the plan uses this style, creating a minor terminological register mismatch.

3. NEAR-DUPLICATE / OVERLAPPING CRITERIA:
   - SC1 and SC2 both cover fail-closed behavior: SC1 requires the gate to be fail-closed on un-reviewed content; SC2 requires fail-closed on precondition/token/expiry failure. The boundary is logically sound but the shared phrase 'fail-closed' is used with two distinct meanings (missing coverage vs. infrastructure failure) without being distinguished in the NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT statement, which may lead to confusion during verification.
   - SC3 duplicates part of SC1: 'live ruleset requires review-gate' (SC3) and 'ruleset-enforced … hard GitHub gate' (SC1/NON-NEGOTIABLE) describe the same durable-ruleset property. The overlap is not harmful but a reviewer could mark SC1 'PASS' while SC3 still fails.

4. DESCRIBED WORK WITHOUT A CRITERION:
   - 'descriptive PR1 titles; sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle' (SC6, UX story 8) is the only mention of PR1 lifecycle behavior; no criterion verifies PR1 title content or draft-umbrella lifecycle correctness beyond a vague 'UX correctness fixes land.'
   - ADR-0020 update is mentioned in SC6 but 'updated with execution results' has no observable/verifiable completion condition attached.
   - The sequencing section describes eight stories and their dependencies (e.g., 'story 5 depends on 1+2+4'), but no success criterion verifies that the sequencing constraints were respected (e.g., story 7 not merged before 1–6 pass).
findings.suggested_edit:
1. Add a definition block at the top of the plan (or in a Glossary section) that defines: PR1, PR2, R8, E13, E-compute-hash, and 'merge-pipeline-checks' so a reader of the ticket alone can resolve all referenced terms.
2. Either add SC7 to close the 'warn-mode validated before enforce-flip' prerequisite, or explicitly fold it into SC3 (e.g., 'SC3 requires evidence that warn-mode ran on ≥N live PR2s before the enforce-flip was merged').
3. Disambiguate 'per-SHA coverage' (SC1) vs. 'coverage ledger' (SC4) — use one term or explicitly state they refer to the same artifact.
4. Make the ADR-0020 update criterion verifiable: e.g., 'ADR-0020 §Results section is populated with actual experiment outcomes before epic closes.'
5. Either remove the course-correction trigger requirement from DELIVERY PRINCIPLES (if it is a process norm, not a gate) or add it as a story-level acceptance check so it is consistently enforced.

findings.location: Success Criteria SC1-SC6 vs. Plan body (Sequencing section + story references)
findings.finding: The SC references named experiments (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a, V0.11, V0.12, R8) and specific story dependencies, but neither the experiment definitions nor the story details are included in this plan text — they live in referenced external documents (docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md, ADR-0020). A reviewer/executor cannot tell from this plan alone what E13 or V0.11 actually require, so the criteria cannot be confirmed to fully map to described work without access to those documents. Additionally, the plan consistently uses 'review-gate' as the check name while also mentioning 'llm-review' and 'merge-pipeline-checks' in SC3 — three different check names that are never jointly defined here, creating potential terminology confusion.
findings.suggested_edit: Either inline a brief definition of every experiment/variant ID referenced in SC1-SC6, or add a legend table mapping each ID to the specific observable/assertion it requires. Clarify whether 'merge-pipeline-checks' is being renamed to 'review-gate' or co-exists alongside it.

findings.location: SC1 ('Verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED'); SC2 ('Verified: a scope-stripped/expired token makes the gate RED'); SC3 ('live ruleset requires review-gate … reflected on BOTH required-checks.txt and the live ruleset'); Delivery Principles ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub')
findings.finding: Multiple success criteria require live GitHub operations (triggering real PRs, stripping OAuth token scopes, reading live rulesets, verifying live merge-pipeline behavior). These cannot be completed by an agent in a single working session without actual GitHub admin credentials, a live repo with active PR2 traffic, and the ability to flip ruleset enforcement modes. SC3 explicitly requires the swap to be 'reflected on … the live ruleset' and SC2 requires a scope-stripped/expired token test — both are real-environment ops that cannot be simulated from plan text or mocked in CI. The 'warn-first / live-verify each story' delivery principle makes in-session completability structurally impossible for the enforce-flip story (Story 7).
findings.suggested_edit: Separate the measurable in-session outputs (script exists, config file correct, unit tests pass) from the live-environment validation gates (block verified on real PR, token expiry tested). Mark the live-verification items as 'manual sign-off steps' with a named owner and explicit instructions, not as automated acceptance criteria an agent is expected to satisfy within a session.

findings.location: SC2 ('ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'); SC3 ('required-checks.txt and the live ruleset'); SC5 (allowlist logic split across carriers)
findings.finding: The plan introduces fail-closed token/precondition logic that must be replicated identically in three separate YAML workflow files. This is a Rule-of-Three violation risk: if the fail-closed wrapper pattern is not extracted into a shared composite action or reusable workflow, each carrier will drift independently. Whether a shared action already exists or a reusable wrapper is planned cannot be confirmed from the plan text alone. Similarly, 'required-checks.txt' as a separate source-of-truth for required checks alongside a live ruleset introduces two places to keep in sync — worth questioning whether the file is necessary if the live ruleset is the authoritative source.
findings.suggested_edit: Explicitly state whether the fail-closed token-check logic will be a shared composite action (DRY) or duplicated per carrier (and if duplicated, justify why). Clarify the role of required-checks.txt: is it a snapshot for drift detection, or a second write target?

findings.location: SC1-SC4 experiment references (E2, E5, E8, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a, R8); Delivery Principles ('named E-series/V-series experiments')
findings.finding: The plan defers all concrete test descriptions to external experiment documents (E-series/V-series) that are not reproduced here. It is therefore impossible to assess whether failure-path cases, boundary cases, and RED-before-GREEN ordering are covered. The plan does not describe, for example: what the warm-hit ledger test looks like (SC4), how the per-SHA coverage boundary is exercised, or how the allowlist exemption for the ticket store is tested independently of the full flow. The use of named experiments is a legitimate pattern, but without their content this criterion is unverifiable from the plan alone.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a concise testing matrix to the plan body listing each AC, its RED case (what should fail and why), its GREEN case, and which experiment/script exercises it. This makes the test plan self-contained without requiring access to the external handoff doc.

findings.location: Sequencing section ('stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel … story 5 depends on 1+2+4 … story 6 depends on 5 … story 7 depends on 1-6'); SC5 ('behavior-bearing surfaces (docs/**, skills, behavior-driving config) are removed from the review-gate allowlist'); SC1 ('no code_changed skip'); Story references throughout
findings.finding: The plan references 8 numbered stories and their dependencies but provides zero content for any individual story — no scope, no file list, no definition of 'done', no owner. An executing agent cannot act on 'story 1-4 may proceed in parallel' without knowing what stories 1-4 actually are. Similarly, SC5 says 'behavior-driving config' is removed from the allowlist but never defines which files are 'behavior-driving config' vs. 'genuinely inert'. 'Skills' is used as a category without definition. The course-correction triggers are mentioned as a named element each story must carry, but no trigger is defined. These are real design decisions deferred to the executor.
findings.suggested_edit: Either inline the 8 story descriptions (even as a brief table: story ID, goal, files touched, done criteria) or link to a section that the executor can read in the same session. Define the allowlist membership rules with a concrete example or file-glob pattern for 'behavior-driving config' vs. 'inert artifacts'.

findings.location: SC3 ('live main ruleset requires review-gate and retains llm-review'); SC2 ('ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'); Delivery Principles ('admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads')
findings.finding: The plan asserts the existence of: (a) a live 'main' ruleset already containing 'llm-review' as a required check, (b) three specific named workflow YAML files, (c) an existing 'required-checks.txt', (d) an existing PAT with admin scope, and (e) an existing two-tier PR1/PR2 flow. None of these are verified within the plan; they are stated as current facts. If any of these does not exist or has a different name, the executing agent will hit unexpected blockers. Access to the live codebase and GitHub repo settings would be needed to confirm.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Preconditions verified' checklist at the top of the plan listing each assumed artifact with the command/UI path to confirm it exists (e.g., 'gh ruleset list --json name | jq .[] | grep llm-review' for the ruleset, 'ls .github/workflows/' for the YAML files).

findings.location: SC3 ('required-checks.txt and the live ruleset'); SC5 ('docs/**, skills, behavior-driving config … removed from review-gate allowlist'); Sequencing ('stories 1-4 … story 8 (UX) is independent')
findings.finding: The plan names several file categories (docs/**, skills, lockfiles, images, ticket store) and three workflow YAMLs, but does not enumerate specific file paths, glob patterns, or which callers/consumers of the existing 'llm-review' check would be affected by the rename/swap to 'review-gate'. If branch protection rules or status-check references exist outside the named ruleset (e.g., in CODEOWNERS, other workflows calling check names by string, third-party integrations), those consumers are unacknowledged. The blast radius of renaming a required check from 'merge-pipeline-checks' to 'review-gate' is non-trivial and not explicitly scoped.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a blast-radius section listing every place the check name 'merge-pipeline-checks' / 'llm-review' is referenced (grep result or manual enumeration), and confirm that the swap story covers all of them.

findings.location: Title vs. body; NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; SC1-SC6
NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; SC1; SC6
findings.finding: The plan clearly states the problem (commits reaching main without LLM review, a flow-enforced gate that can be silently bypassed) and the beneficiary (the team relying on the two-tier review flow for correctness assurance). The 'warn-first' and course-correction framing also implicitly names the team as stakeholder.

findings.location: Sequencing section ('stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 depends on 1-6; story 8 is independent')
findings.finding: This is an epic containing 8 stories, but no individual story is described anywhere in the plan text. The plan is thus simultaneously too large (8 stories worth of work) and too thin (zero leaf-level detail). For a parent/epic plan the expectation is that child tickets are linked or at minimum summarized. As written, an agent would have no idea where to start within any individual story. The 'may proceed in parallel' sequencing instruction is especially problematic without story content — it implies multiple agents or sessions, but without story boundaries there is no way to partition the work.
findings.suggested_edit: Create (or link to already-created) child tickets for each of the 8 stories with at minimum: goal statement, file scope, done criteria, and the named course-correction trigger. The epic plan should summarize, not substitute for, those children.

findings.location: Delivery Principles ('Warn-first: every story ships its enforcing logic in warn/advisory mode … BEFORE any enforce-flip') vs. SC2 ('all review-enforcing CI wrappers fail-closed on precondition/token failure under enforce mode … in ALL THREE carriers'); SC3 ('merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap is reflected on BOTH required-checks.txt AND the live ruleset')
findings.finding: Two coherence tensions: (1) The warn-first principle states enforcing logic ships in warn mode first, but SC2 as written describes the fail-closed behavior as a success criterion without specifying it applies only after the enforce-flip (Story 7). An executor reading SC2 in isolation could interpret it as requiring fail-closed behavior to be present from Story 1, contradicting the warn-first principle. (2) SC3 requires the swap to be reflected in 'required-checks.txt AND the live ruleset' — two writes — but the Delivery Principles warn about a 'live<->file skew window' as a risk, implying the two can be out of sync; this tension is acknowledged but the plan does not specify which is authoritative during the window.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a note to SC2 clarifying it is the post-enforce-flip state, not a day-1 requirement. For SC3, explicitly name which file is authoritative during the skew window and how long the window is expected to last.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA header ('epic closes only when ALL are met + completion-verifier PASS'); SC1-SC6 experiment references; Sequencing ('story 7 (enforce-flip) depends on 1-6 ALL verified')
findings.finding: The plan declares a 'completion-verifier PASS' as required for epic closure but does not define what the completion-verifier is, who runs it, or what it checks. SC1-SC5 defer verification to external experiment IDs (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.11) whose content is not in this document. An agent cannot determine when to stop without knowing: (a) what the completion-verifier script/check does, (b) where the experiment definitions live and how to run them, and (c) the specific observable that constitutes 'PASS' for each SC. The end-state is named but the path to reach it is not self-contained.
findings.suggested_edit: Define the completion-verifier inline: what command runs it, what its PASS output looks like, and who is responsible for signing off. For each SC, add a concrete terminal command or UI state that constitutes verification (e.g., 'gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets | jq … returns review-gate in required_status_checks').

findings.location: Plan title vs. NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT, SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1-SC6, and SEQUENCING sections
Multiple sections of the plan description and acceptance criteria
findings.finding:
The plan references external documents as the primary source of executable detail ('Source of truth: docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md … and docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md') but does not reproduce or summarize the concrete specifications from those documents. An executing agent must open those files to know what to build. If those files are absent, inaccessible, or ambiguous themselves, the agent has no fallback. Specific gaps that surface without those documents:

1. **Story definitions are entirely absent.** The plan mentions 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel', 'story 5 depends on 1+2+4', etc., but no story is defined in the ticket — no title, no scope, no done-criteria. The agent cannot start work.

2. **'review-gate' check implementation is unspecified.** SC1 requires a single always-runs required check named `review-gate` but the plan never states what script/action/workflow implements it, where it lives, what its inputs/outputs are, or what 'per-SHA coverage proven reviewed' means concretely.

3. **'per-SHA coverage' ledger is unspecified.** SC1 and SC4 reference a 'coverage ledger' and 'warm-hits across re-runs' but no schema, storage location, TTL, or update protocol is given.

4. **'merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap' is opaque.** SC3 says this swap must be reflected in both `required-checks.txt` and the live ruleset, but no format for `required-checks.txt`, no API call sequence, and no idempotency/ordering constraint is provided.

5. **Allowlist contents are undefined.** SC5 states that 'behavior-bearing surfaces' are removed from the review-gate allowlist and 'genuinely-inert artifacts … remain exempt', but the allowlist file/format/current contents are not named in the plan.

6. **'R8 RED is repo-wide during the live<->file skew window'** — 'R8' is referenced as a known artifact but not defined in this ticket; an agent unfamiliar with the external docs cannot reason about the runbook.

7. **Experiment references (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, V0.12) are cited as verification steps** but the pass/fail criteria for each experiment are not included; the agent cannot know when SC verification is complete.

8. **'warn-mode' vs 'enforce-mode' toggle mechanics** are described conceptually but the actual flag name, file location, and change procedure are not stated anywhere in the plan.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a self-contained 'Story Definitions' section that enumerates each story (1–8) with: title, scope (files/workflows to create or modify), concrete done-criteria, and the specific experiments that verify it. Inline or append the minimal normative content from option-a-pivot-plan.md and ADR-0020 that the agent needs — at minimum: the `review-gate` workflow skeleton, the coverage-ledger schema, the allowlist file path and current contents, the warn/enforce toggle mechanic (flag name + location), and a one-paragraph description of each named experiment (E-series/V-series) including its pass condition. Without this, the plan cannot be executed without the agent first reading and interpreting two external documents.

findings.location: Multiple sections: SEQUENCING ('stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 is independent'); SC1-SC6; NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES
findings.finding:
The epic-level plan references 8 numbered stories (S1–S8) and their dependency graph, named experiments (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, E-compute-hash), named variants (V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11), and named checks/runbook artefacts (R8, required-checks.txt, review-gate, llm-review, review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml) — but NONE of these stories, experiments, or artefacts are defined or quoted inside the plan itself. The sole source of truth is cited as two external documents (`docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md` and `docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md`) whose contents are not reproduced here. An executing agent would need to:
1. Locate and parse those docs before it can do anything (the plan gives no fallback if they are absent, stale, or not yet written).
2. Infer what each story's concrete scope, deliverables, and done-criteria are — the plan names stories only by number, with no description per story.
3. Resolve all experiment/variant IDs (e.g. 'E-compute-hash', 'V0.11') that are referenced in the success criteria but never explained.
4. Determine what 'the live<->file skew window' means operationally for the R8 runbook step, which is described only as 'land the swap with no other PR2 in flight' — no steps, rollback commands, or time-box are given.
5. Decide what 'COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER' means concretely for each story (the plan says each story names one, but no triggers are listed here).
6. Determine what PAT scopes, expiry handling, and token-injection mechanisms are required (SC2 says 'scope-stripped/expired token makes the gate RED' but gives no spec for how that is implemented or tested).
7. Understand the 'allowlisted-only PR2' concept in SC1 and what the allowlist contains — no list or schema is provided.
These are not minor gaps; they are the entire execution-level specification. The plan as written is an index/summary of work defined elsewhere, not a self-contained, executable specification. An agent cannot start Story 1 (let alone proceed in parallel on 1–4) without access to the referenced source documents and story definitions.
findings.suggested_edit: Either (a) inline the story definitions (scope, deliverables, acceptance tests, course-correction trigger) for each of S1–S8 directly into this ticket, referencing the external docs only for background rationale; or (b) split this epic into per-story child tickets, each fully self-contained, and make this ticket the parent/index only (explicitly marking it non-executable). Also: resolve every experiment/variant ID reference in SC1–SC6 by either defining them inline or linking to an anchored, versioned location. For the R8 runbook, provide at minimum: pre-flight checks, the exact swap command/PR sequence, rollback steps, and time-box.

findings.location: Multiple sections of the plan description and all six Success Criteria
findings.finding:
The epic-level plan as written is a roadmap/framing document, not a self-contained executable specification. An agent attempting to act on it would need to stop and ask clarifying questions at several concrete decision points:

1. **Story content is absent.** The plan references 'stories 1-4', 'story 5 depends on 1+2+4', 'story 6 depends on 5', 'story 7 (enforce-flip)', 'story 8 (UX)' — but none of the individual story cards with their own acceptance criteria, file paths, commands, or implementation steps appear anywhere in this ticket. An agent cannot execute 'story 1' without knowing what it is.

2. **Source-of-truth documents are referenced but not quoted.** 'docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md (CONVERGED, review #1-6 + experiments V0.1-V0.12)' and 'docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md' are cited as the authoritative specifications, but their content is not included. An agent has no way to know what '§5 Phase 2' or 'review-#4/#6 prerequisites' prescribe without reading those files — which requires codebase access and creates an implicit dependency.

3. **'R8 RED' and 'live<->file skew window' are undefined.** SC3 and the Reversibility bullet reference 'R8 RED' and 'the live<->file skew window' as operational concepts, but neither is explained. An agent would not know what R8 is, what its RED state means operationally, or how long the skew window is.

4. **'E-series/V-series experiments' are named but not defined.** SC1–SC5 cite E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8, E-compute-hash as verification steps, but the experiments themselves (what to run, expected output, pass/fail criteria) are not described anywhere in this ticket. An agent cannot 'verify live' without knowing what to run.

5. **'completion-verifier PASS' is undefined.** SC intro reads 'epic closes only when ALL are met + completion-verifier PASS' — the completion-verifier is not named, located, or described.

6. **'admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads'** — the plan instructs live-verify with these PATs but does not specify where the PATs come from, which secrets names to use, or what 'provision/read' vs 'containment reads' means precisely.

7. **'warn-mode vs enforce-mode flip'** — the Warn-first principle states 'every story ships its enforcing logic in warn/advisory mode … BEFORE any enforce-flip,' but the boundary between warn-mode and enforce-mode behavior is only described at a high level. There is no specification of the exact flag, env-var, or config value that controls the mode, nor where it lives in each of the three carriers (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml).
findings.suggested_edit:
Before this plan is executed, each of the following gaps should be resolved either in this ticket or by linking to a pre-existing, agent-readable artifact:
- Expand or inline each story card (1–8) with its own scope, acceptance criteria, and file-level changes.
- Either inline the relevant sections of docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md and ADR-0020, or confirm the agent has filesystem access and specify the exact section references.
- Define 'R8', its RED/GREEN semantics, and the skew-window duration.
- For each cited experiment (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13–E15, V0.3a, V0.11, E-compute-hash, R8) add: command to run, expected stdout/exit-code for pass, expected for fail.
- Name the completion-verifier (script path or CI job name) and describe its pass criteria.
- Name the secrets/env-vars for admin and non-admin PATs and clarify 'provision/read' vs 'containment reads'.
- Specify the exact mechanism (env-var name, config key, or CLI flag) that switches each CI wrapper between warn and enforce mode.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC6, DELIVERY PRINCIPLES (warn-first / live-verify), and the referenced experiment tags E2/E5/E8/E11/E13/E14/E15/V0.3a-post-fix/V0.11/R8
findings.finding:
The plan names a rich set of experiment and verification tags (E2, E5, E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8) and maps each success criterion to specific experiments, which is a strong signal that a testing plan exists. However, the actual test definitions live in the external documents (`docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md` and `docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md`) that are not reproduced here. Without seeing those documents it is impossible to confirm that:
1. Every named experiment covers a distinct failure mode (RED-before-GREEN is asserted in the principles but not shown per-story).
2. Boundary / failure-path tests are specified for the token-expiry and scope-strip paths in all three carriers (SC2 mentions this, but the concrete test steps are presumably only in the external docs).
3. The coverage-ledger warm-hit test (SC4/E14/E15) has a specific observable and a definition of 'pass' (no timing/re-run budget stated in the ticket).
4. The docs-only PR and image/lockfile PR smoke-tests (SC5/E11) are written as reproducible steps rather than one-off manual checks.

The plan's DELIVERY PRINCIPLES say 'live-verify each story on real GitHub … by the named E-series/V-series experiments', but per-story test steps and RED-before-GREEN checkpoints are not inlined here — they are delegated entirely to the external source-of-truth documents. If those documents are complete and available to the executing agent, this is adequate; if not, the agent has no local specification of test ordering or failure observables.
findings.suggested_edit: For each story, inline at minimum: (a) the specific observable that constitutes a RED (failing) result before the fix/feature lands, (b) the specific observable that constitutes a GREEN after, and (c) the failure-path check (e.g., 'scope-stripped PAT → gate exits non-zero') as a named step rather than a reference to an external experiment tag. This makes the testing plan self-contained and removes the dependency on external docs being accessible to the reviewer/agent.

findings.location: Multiple sections throughout the plan description and all six Success Criteria
findings.finding:
The epic-level plan as presented is a high-level orchestration document, not an immediately executable story. An agent attempting to act on it would encounter at least eight distinct stopping points requiring clarification:

1. **Story bodies are absent.** The plan references 'stories 1–4', 'story 5', 'story 6', 'story 7 (enforce-flip)', and 'story 8 (UX)' but does not include the actual story text, file paths to change, commands to run, or acceptance tests. An agent cannot write, test, or merge anything without those details.

2. **Source-of-truth documents are not reproduced.** The plan says 'Source of truth: docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md … and docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md' but neither document's content is included. An agent must either access the repo (not guaranteed) or stop and ask.

3. **'warn-mode' vs 'enforce-mode' toggle is unspecified.** SC2 requires all CI wrappers to fail-closed under 'enforce mode', and the plan says 'every story ships its enforcing logic in warn/advisory mode … BEFORE any enforce-flip.' The mechanism for toggling (env var? workflow input? branch protection flag?) is never defined anywhere in the plan.

4. **Ruleset mutation details are absent.** SC3 says 'the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap is reflected on BOTH required-checks.txt and the live ruleset' but gives no GitHub ruleset ID, no API payload, no example of required-checks.txt, and no instruction on how to perform the swap atomically ('land the swap with no other PR2 in flight').

5. **Allowlist contents are underspecified.** SC1 references 'an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content' and SC5 references 'the review-gate allowlist', but the plan never defines what the allowlist is, where it lives, or its current contents — only that 'behavior-bearing surfaces … are removed from it'.

6. **Experiment references are opaque decision gates.** Every SC cites experiments (E2, E13, V0.3a-post-fix, E6', V0.11, E5, E8, E14, E15, E11, E-compute-hash, R8) as verification steps. None of these are described in the plan body. An agent cannot run or interpret them without access to the source-of-truth docs.

7. **COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER is described generically.** The plan says 'each story names a COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER' but, because story bodies are absent, no specific triggers exist in this document. An agent proceeding story-by-story cannot know when to halt.

8. **'completion-verifier PASS' is undefined.** The epic close condition says 'epic closes only when ALL are met + completion-verifier PASS' — the verifier script/tool/process is not named or described.
findings.suggested_edit: Before assigning this epic to an executing agent, expand it to include: (a) the full text of each story (1–8) with explicit file paths, commands, and per-story ACs; (b) inline the relevant sections of option-a-pivot-plan.md and ADR-0020 or attach them as appendices; (c) name the warn/enforce toggle mechanism (e.g., 'set ENFORCE=true in the workflow env or pass --enforce flag to the gate script'); (d) provide the GitHub ruleset ID and the exact API call / UI step for the swap; (e) define the allowlist file location and its current contents; (f) describe each cited experiment (E/V/R series) in enough detail that an agent can execute and evaluate them; (g) write out the COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER for each story as a concrete observable (e.g., 'if review-gate exits 0 on a PR with un-reviewed SHA X, stop'); (h) name the completion-verifier (e.g., 'scripts/verify-epic-close.sh') and describe what it checks.

findings.location: SEQUENCING section + SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC6
SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC6; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES; references to E-series/V-series experiments; story dependency descriptions
findings.finding:
The plan names a rich set of experiments (E2, E5, E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8, E-compute-hash) and describes expected outcomes (BLOCKED, RED, warm-hit, skip) at the epic level, which is a strong signal of test intent. However, the per-story testing detail is not visible in this plan excerpt — the individual stories are referenced but not reproduced here. Specifically:

1. RED-before-GREEN ordering: The plan mandates warn-first → enforce-flip sequencing (story 7 depends on stories 1–6 all verified), which structurally enforces a RED-before-GREEN discipline. This is PRESENT at the epic level.

2. Failure-path coverage: SC2 names all three CI carrier files and specifies a scope-stripped/expired token scenario, and SC3 names a live-parity guard for PAT expiry/scope-strip/drift. These are concrete failure-path assertions. PRESENT.

3. Behavioral/contractual coverage: SC1 (un-reviewed SHA blocked), SC4 (clean PR passes, exempt commit does not wedge, ledger warm-hits across re-runs), SC5 (docs-only PR requires review; image/lockfile PR skips) are behavioral contract assertions. PRESENT.

4. Boundary cases: SC4 covers the 'entirely within event-sourced ticket store' exemption boundary and 'flaky-check' re-run boundary. SC5 covers the inert-artifact boundary. PRESENT at a high level.

5. What cannot be assessed without the story files: Each story is described as naming its own COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER and E-series/V-series experiments, but those per-story test steps and acceptance checks are in the referenced story artifacts (not reproduced here). It is therefore AMBIGUOUS whether every story independently specifies: (a) the exact command/assertion to confirm RED vs GREEN, (b) which actor (admin PAT vs non-admin) runs which check, and (c) a documented rollback / abort condition that is testable. The plan references docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md and docs/adr/0020 as sources of truth but those are not available for review here.

6. Change-detector avoidance: The coverage-ledger 'warm-hit across re-runs' in SC4 (E14/E15) addresses flakiness, but the plan does not explicitly describe how the experiments distinguish a genuine coverage failure from a ledger-cache miss — this boundary condition is named but not specified in testable terms within this excerpt.
findings.suggested_edit: For completeness, consider adding to each story's AC (or to a shared testing-appendix section in the epic): (a) the precise shell invocation or CI-log assertion that constitutes PASS/FAIL for that story's experiment, (b) which PAT scope (admin vs non-admin) is used for each live-verify step, and (c) an explicit 'ledger-miss vs genuine-failure distinguishability' note for SC4/E14/E15 — e.g., 'warm-hit is confirmed when the second run of the same SHA produces exit-0 in under N seconds without re-calling the LLM endpoint'.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA (SC1–SC6), DELIVERY PRINCIPLES (warn-first / live-verify), and the references to E-series/V-series experiments throughout the plan
findings.finding: The plan names a rich set of experiment identifiers (E2, E5, E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, E-compute-hash, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8) as verification anchors for each success criterion, which is a positive signal for testing completeness. However, the plan itself never defines what each experiment/variant actually does — what input it provides, what observable output constitutes PASS, and critically, what the RED-before-GREEN ordering looks like for each. The definitions presumably live in docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md and docs/adr/0020, which are referenced as source-of-truth but are not reproduced here. Without access to those documents it is impossible to confirm whether the named experiments together cover: (a) failure-path / precondition failures for all three CI carriers (SC2); (b) boundary cases such as per-SHA partial coverage and the coverage-ledger warm-hit (SC4); (c) the allowlist boundary — inert vs. behavior-bearing — for the review-gate (SC5); (d) RED-before-GREEN ordering (i.e., that each test is first run against a known-bad state to confirm it catches the regression before the fix is applied). SC4 references E5 ('clean fully-reviewed PR2 passes') and E8/E14/E15 (ledger warm-hit / no-flaky-check), but there is no description of a corresponding RED case (e.g., 'ledger cache poisoned → expect RED'). Similarly, SC1 references E2/E13 for the blocked-PR case but does not call out a complementary PASS case beyond 'clean PR2 passes' in SC4, leaving it ambiguous whether the same experiment double-checks both legs. The COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER named per-story is a good safeguard, but it is an operational escape hatch rather than a test specification. If the experiment definitions in the referenced docs are comprehensive and do cover the above gaps, this becomes a PASS; the ambiguity is entirely due to those docs being out of scope for this review.
findings.suggested_edit: In each story's acceptance criteria (or in a dedicated 'Test Plan' subsection), explicitly state: (1) the RED state that each named experiment starts from (what deliberate breakage is introduced), (2) the exact observable that flips it GREEN, and (3) at least one boundary/failure-path case per CI carrier (token-expired, scope-stripped, precondition missing). This makes the test plan self-contained without requiring readers to cross-reference docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md. Example for SC2: 'V0.3a-post-fix: start with a scope-stripped PAT → confirm review-coverage-invariant.yml exits non-zero (RED); restore PAT → confirm exit-0 (GREEN). Repeat for dangling-references.yml and ruleset-invariants.yml.'

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA (SC1–SC6), DELIVERY PRINCIPLES (Warn-first / Live-verify), and referenced experiment tags E2, E5, E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, R8
findings.finding:
The plan references a rich set of named experiments and variants (E2, E5, E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8, E-compute-hash) but does not reproduce their content — it relies entirely on the external documents `docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md` and `docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md`. Without access to those documents it is impossible to confirm that the referenced experiments together deliver the required test-plan properties:

1. **RED-before-GREEN ordering**: SC1 asserts that an allowlisted-only PR with un-reviewed content must be BLOCKED (not skip-to-success). There is no in-plan step that explicitly runs the gate in a RED state before flipping to enforce mode to confirm the gate can actually fire RED.
2. **Failure-path / precondition coverage**: SC2 names three CI carriers (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml) and three failure modes (scope-stripped token, expired token, general precondition failure). The plan states these are verified by V0.3a-post-fix and R8, but the specific test steps — how the token is stripped/expired, which runner executes, what exit code is asserted — are not visible in the plan text.
3. **Boundary / change-detector avoidance**: SC4 mentions 'coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs (no flaky-check team block)' and references E5/E8/E14/E15, but the plan does not describe the mechanism for asserting warm-hit idempotency (e.g. a repeated run on the same SHA set must exit-0 without re-calling the LLM) or how flakiness is detected vs. tolerated.
4. **Story-level test hooks**: Individual stories are referenced (stories 1–8) but are not expanded in the plan text, so it is not verifiable whether each story ships its own warn-mode validation step before the enforce-flip, or whether that validation is only done at the epic level in story 7.
5. **Positive path for SC5**: A docs-only PR and an image/lockfile PR are named as test cases, but there is no description of how the result is asserted (e.g. CI log grep, required-check badge colour, manual inspection) or who is responsible for executing the assertion.

Because all detailed test procedures are deferred to the referenced external documents — which are not part of this plan and not available for review — a definitive PASS or FAIL cannot be rendered. The plan as written is a summary/epic-level document, not the full test spec, so the gap is structural rather than an outright omission. However, the plan should either inline the critical failure-path assertions for each SC, or explicitly state where those procedures live and confirm they are present in the referenced docs.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Testing summary' sub-section in the plan (or per-story) that, for each SC, explicitly states: (a) the concrete RED-before-GREEN step (e.g. 'submit a PR whose review record is absent; assert review-gate exits 1 before story-7 enforce-flip'), (b) how token-failure is induced and what exit code is expected for each of the three CI carriers, and (c) how warm-hit idempotency is asserted (e.g. 'run coverage check twice on same HEAD SHA; assert second run exits 0 with cache-hit log line and no new LLM call'). Reference the specific experiment document section rather than just the experiment tag so a reader can verify the procedure without hunting across docs.

findings.finding:
The plan articulates six success criteria and references a 'completion-verifier PASS' as the final epic gate, but the plan never defines what the completion-verifier is, what it checks, or who/what runs it. Without knowing what constitutes a PASS signal from this verifier, there is no clear termination condition at the epic level. Individual stories are sequenced correctly (1-4 → 5 → 6 → 7 → 8 independent), and each SC maps to named experiments (E2, E13, V0.3a, etc.), which is good. However, three gaps undermine end-state reachability:

1. **Completion-verifier undefined**: 'epic closes only when ALL are met + completion-verifier PASS' — the verifier is never described (script? manual checklist? automated CI job? who owns it?). This is a termination-condition gap.

2. **Experiment references without results**: SC1 references [E2/E13], SC2 references [V0.3a-post-fix, R8 fail-closed], etc. The plan cross-references these but the experiments' pass/fail criteria and where their results are recorded are not specified in the plan itself. If the referenced doc (docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md) contains them, that's fine — but the plan gives no pointer to how those experiment results map to the SC verdicts during execution, creating a verification gap for the agent.

3. **SC3 end-state reachability gap**: SC3 requires the live ruleset to require both `review-gate` AND retain `llm-review`, and for `required-checks.txt` to match the live ruleset. The plan does not describe a step (in any named story) that explicitly validates post-swap live/file parity under the R8 guard in enforce mode — it is asserted as a success criterion but the story that produces this proof is not identified. The R8 live-parity guard is mentioned in SC2 and SC3 but the story that wires it in enforce mode is not called out in the visible sequencing.

These gaps mean an agent could complete all described stories and still be unable to determine whether the epic is closed, or could miss that SC3's parity-guard flip to enforce mode requires its own explicit story step.
findings.suggested_edit:
1. Define the completion-verifier explicitly: name the script or checklist (e.g., 'scripts/verify-epic-sc.sh checks SC1–SC6 by running the named experiments against the live repo and outputs a structured PASS/FAIL report; a PASS report is required before the epic is closed').
2. For each SC, add a one-line 'proof artifact' pointer: e.g., 'SC1 verified by: output of E2 run stored in docs/experiments/E2-result.md showing BLOCKED status'.
3. In the SEQUENCING section, add an explicit step after story 7 (enforce-flip): 'Story 9 (or story 7 post-step): run completion-verifier; confirm R8 guard is in enforce mode and live/file parity is clean. This is the formal close gate.' This removes the ambiguity about when the epic terminates.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA (SC1–SC6), DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story…'), and individual story references to E-series/V-series experiments
findings.finding:
The plan gestures at a rich test corpus (E2, E5, E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8 fail-closed, etc.) but the actual test specifications — what exactly each experiment/variant does, what observable output constitutes PASS vs FAIL, and what the RED-before-GREEN ordering is — are entirely deferred to the referenced documents (docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md and ADR-0020). Within the plan text itself:

1. FAILURE-PATH / FAIL-CLOSED coverage: SC2 names 'a scope-stripped/expired token makes the gate RED' and references V0.3a-post-fix/R8, but the plan never states how that scenario is exercised (synthetic token injection? CI env-var wipe? manual PAT revocation?) nor which script/workflow implements it. An agent implementing this from the plan alone cannot know what artifact to produce.

2. RED-before-GREEN ordering: The warn-first principle implies each story must be observed RED in advisory mode before the enforce-flip (story 7), but no story explicitly states 'the following must be seen RED on live PR2 traffic before proceeding', nor names the expected RED observable. Story 7's dependency ('1-6 ALL verified') is asserted but the verification contract is opaque.

3. Boundary / edge-case test completeness for SC4: SC4 references E5/E8/E14/E15 for 'no false wedge' and 'coverage ledger warm-hits', but the plan does not describe what the ledger warm-hit test looks like (e.g. run twice and assert cache hit log line?) nor what 'flaky-check' scenario is being guarded against.

4. SC5 allowlist boundary test: 'a docs-only PR now requires review; an image/lockfile PR still skips' — the plan doesn't specify whether these are automated CI tests, manual spot-checks, or experiment replays, making it impossible to confirm the test will be machine-verifiable.

The plan is not fatally incomplete — the referenced documents may supply all missing detail — but because those documents are not part of the plan under review, an agent executing only from this ticket cannot reconstruct a complete, self-contained test plan. The ambiguity is whether the experiment specifications in the linked docs are sufficient and up-to-date with the current scope.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Test Plan' section (or expand each story's acceptance criteria) that, for every Success Criterion, states: (a) the specific command / workflow / script that exercises it, (b) the exact observable that constitutes RED and GREEN, and (c) when in the story sequence it must be run. At minimum, inline the key experiment definitions for E2, E5, E8, E13, V0.3a-post-fix, and R8 so the plan is self-contained. Example for SC2: 'Exercise: in CI, set GITHUB_TOKEN to a token with no repo scope and run review-coverage-invariant.yml; assert exit code ≠ 0 and that the job summary contains the string "fail-closed: token validation failed".'

findings.location: SEQUENCING section and overall plan structure
SUCCESS CRITERIA (SC1–SC6), SEQUENCING section, and individual story references (E2/E13, V0.3a-post-fix, R8, E6', V0.11, E5/E8/E14/E15, E11/E-compute-hash)
findings.finding:
The plan names a 'completion-verifier PASS' as the final gate for the epic closing (SUCCESS CRITERIA preamble: 'epic closes only when ALL are met + completion-verifier PASS'), but the completion-verifier itself is never defined in the plan text. There is no description of: (a) what artifact or script the completion-verifier is, (b) who runs it, (c) what its pass/fail output looks like, or (d) where its result is recorded. Without this definition it is impossible to know when 'done' is officially reached.

Additionally, the verification steps for each SC rely entirely on named experiments and review labels (E2, E13, V0.3a-post-fix, R8, E6', V0.11, etc.) that are defined in external documents ('docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md', 'docs/adr/0020-…') not reproduced here. A reviewer or executing agent reading only this ticket cannot independently confirm what observable evidence satisfies each SC without access to those documents. This is a reachability gap: the union of steps described in THIS plan is insufficient on its own to confirm the end-state without resolving the external references.

The SEQUENCING section establishes a dependency graph (stories 1–4 parallel → 5 → 6 → 7 enforce-flip; story 8 independent) which is coherent and does provide a termination structure. The COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER requirement per story is a positive signal for stopping criteria, but those triggers are described as living in individual stories — none of which are written out in this plan document. So the stop/go logic for each intermediate phase is also externally defined and unverifiable here.

Net assessment: the high-level termination logic (all SCs met + completion-verifier PASS + story dependency chain exhausted) is structurally sound, but two concrete gaps prevent a PASS verdict: (1) the completion-verifier is undefined in the plan, and (2) the experiment/review-label evidence required to confirm each SC is entirely housed in external documents that are not summarised here.
findings.suggested_edit:
1. Add a short 'Completion Verifier' subsection directly in this plan that names the script/checklist (e.g., 'scripts/verify-epic-sc.sh' or a named checklist in the PR description), its expected output format, and who is responsible for running it before the epic is closed.
2. For each SC, inline the minimum observable evidence required (e.g., for SC1: 'GitHub branch-protection UI shows review-gate as required; a test PR with un-reviewed content shows status=failure on review-gate check') so the plan is self-contained enough for an agent to verify without accessing the external handoff doc.
3. Optionally, add a one-line summary of each referenced experiment (E2, E13, V0.3a-post-fix, etc.) as a tooltip or parenthetical so the termination signal per SC is unambiguous within this document.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA (SC1–SC6), SEQUENCING section, and story-level references (E2/E13, V0.3a-post-fix, R8, E6', V0.11, E5/E8/E14/E15, E11/E-compute-hash)
findings.finding: The plan names a rich set of verification experiments (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.11, V0.3a-post-fix, R8, E-compute-hash) and references a 'completion-verifier PASS' as the ultimate gate, but the body of the ticket does not define: (1) what each experiment/invariant check actually does or measures, (2) what observable output constitutes PASS vs FAIL for each, (3) where the completion-verifier lives and who runs it, or (4) the specific terminal state (e.g., exact required-checks.txt diff, exact ruleset fields, exact exit-code behavior) that an agent can mechanically confirm. The story-level dependency chain (stories 1→5→6→7, story 8 independent) is stated but the stories themselves are not included in the plan text, so it is impossible to confirm that the union of story steps closes every SC gap. Concretely: SC1 says 'an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED' — but the allowlist definition, what 'proven reviewed' means per-SHA, and the exact check name & repo location are not stated.
